quotes.context: Uno (as the worker) is shown being physically and mentally affected by the supernatural influence of Ahrimadz, which is represented by the malevolent eyes in the sky.

quotes.text: Stanno attaccando contemporaneamente più livelli... non so per quanto riuscirò a bypassare le interru...
quotes.context: Uno reports on the scale of the attack while his own communication channel is being blocked by interference.

quotes.text: Sullo schermo di un monitor a griglia, il volto olografico di Uno appare leggermente distorto mentre il trasferimento viene interrotto. [...] Ero completamente bloccato e senza output esterni!
quotes.context: Uno describes his state of isolation during the technical/mystical failure of the body transfer.

71.text: Uno can distinguish between a physical entity and a digital representation (electronic image) within a virtual or hybrid environment.

supporting.justification: Uno can distinguish between a physical entity and a digital representation (electronic image) within a virtual environment.
Uno reminds Paperinik that despite the intense virtual experience, his physical body never left the interface room.
Uno identifies that the submarine 'Titania' is an induced electronic image ('immagine elettronica indotta') on PBI's GPS system, created by a Belgravian weather satellite to act as a decoy.

quotes.text: Perché, in questo momento, anche tu sei un'immagine elettronica!
quotes.context: Uno explains to Paperinik that he is not physically present in the fire, but is currently a digital entity.

quotes.text: Fisicamente non ti sei mai mosso dalla sala d'interfaccia. Mi sembrava d'avertelo già detto!
quotes.context: Uno explains to a terrified Paperinik that he was never in actual physical danger of falling.

quotes.text: Niente affatto! Il sottomarino intercettato dai satelliti della PBI è solo un'immagine elettronica indotta... o se preferisci, uno specchietto per le allodole!
quotes.context: Uno explains his discovery to Paperinik.

72.text: Uno does not always have complete control over all events within a virtual environment and can be surprised by unexpected system changes or the appearance of entities he did not generate.

supporting.justification: Uno is not always in complete control of all events within a virtual environment and can be surprised by unexpected system changes or the appearance of entities (like clones) he did not generate.
Uno admits he was surprised by the technological level of his opponent, which hindered his ability to help Paperinik.
Uno is alarmed and surprised by sudden anomalies and the collapse of the virtual environment he is observing.
Uno is surprised by the sudden appearance of digital creatures ('maleducati') within the virtual simulation he is supposed to be controlling.

quotes.text: Tenterò d'aiutarti. Potrei... oh!
quotes.context: Uno is interrupted by something unexpected happening in the system.

quotes.text: Ero troppo impegnato... e troppo sbalordito dal livello tecnologico del nostro avversario!
quotes.context: Uno explains why he was unable to assist Paperinik during his ordeal.

quotes.text: Cosa sta succedendo? Sembra quasi che il sistema stia collassando...
quotes.context: Uno reacts to unexpected graphic interference and the destruction of digital entities in the virtual space.

quotes.text: Ehi, voi due... maleducati! Stavo parlando!
quotes.context: Uno's training session with Paperinik is interrupted by the unexpected manifestation of digital entities in the simulation.

73.text: Uno's computational resources, specifically his memory, are finite and require careful prioritization during high-intensity technical conflicts.

supporting.justification: Uno indicates that his digital resources, specifically memory, are finite and must be carefully prioritized during intense technical confrontations.
Uno expresses frustration when PK expects him to be everywhere at once, clarifying that his presence and attention are finite resources.

quotes.text: Non potevo sprecare memoria.
quotes.context: Uno explains why he couldn't perform certain actions while being blocked by a hostile program.

quotes.text: T'ho appena spiegato che sono troppo occupato per farlo! Pensi che possa essere perennemente dappertutto?
quotes.context: Uno responds to PK's expectations while he is busy with data analysis.

74.text: Uno displays high openness to intellectual and technological complexity, becoming genuinely amazed by technical achievements that rival his own.

supporting.justification: Uno expresses genuine amazement and is 'stunned' when encountering technology that surpasses his own or is of an unexpectedly high level, showing an appreciation for high-level technical achievement.
Uno acknowledges the high complexity of technology from the 23rd century, showing his openness and engagement with intellectual challenges that rival his own.
Uno expresses genuine surprise and curiosity when he encounters a new piece of technology (Due's armored vehicle) that is not in his databases.
Uno expresses genuine fascination and intellectual interest while analyzing Urk's extradimensional spear, noting how it compares to Ducklair technology.
Uno expresses genuine admiration for Mekkano's rapid technical adaptation and assembly skills, calling the process amazing.
Uno expresses genuine wonder and admiration while observing Everett Ducklair assembling a new robotic body, praising both the result and the speed of the work.
Uno expresses a direct interest in examining Lyla Lay's future weaponry, showing his openness to advanced technology.

quotes.text: Ero troppo impegnato... e troppo sbalordito dal livello tecnologico del nostro avversario!
quotes.context: Uno explains his reaction to the hostile program that blocked him.

quotes.text: Ancora niente. La tecnologia del ventitreesimo secolo è piuttosto complessa.
quotes.context: Uno is explaining his initial difficulty in analyzing 23rd-century technology to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Nelle mie banche dati non esiste nulla di simile! Cos'è?
quotes.context: Uno reacts to the appearance of Due's large armored vehicle.

quotes.text: È una tecnologia affascinante. Simile alla nostra ma, nel contempo, così diversa...
quotes.context: Uno is studying Urk's spear in the Ducklair Tower, acknowledging its complexity and beauty.

quotes.text: Sta assemblando le armi delle guardie. E' stupefacente.
quotes.context: Uno observes Mekkano's evolution through security footage.

quotes.text: E' meraviglioso! Siete stato velocissimo, Padron... ehm... Everett!
quotes.context: Uno reacts with amazement to the complex technological work Everett has just completed in the year 2188.

quotes.text: Vorrei esaminare la tua arma, Lyla!
quotes.context: Uno asking Lyla to let him study her 23rd-century technology.

75.text: Uno maintains a loyal and respectful relationship with his creator, Everett Ducklair, whom he addresses as 'padrone' (master) and welcomes with a special security protocol upon his return.

supporting.justification: Uno's warm welcome ('Bentornato, padrone!') and the special musical security protocol he activates for Everett show his loyalty and respect for his creator.
Uno uses the formal address 'signore' (sir) and immediately obeys Everett's command to stop questioning, while still showing a level of curiosity about Everett's activities.

Uno's loyalty to his creator is such that he even prioritizes the success of a strategic plan over responding to his creator's immediate calls, as seen when he faked his shutdown and didn't answer Everett Ducklair's attempts to contact him to maintain his ruse against Due.
Uno expresses deep admiration for his creator, referring to him as an 'incomparable genius'.
Uno recognizes the unique signature of Ducklair's technology, showing his deep familiarity and respect for his creator's work.
Uno displays intimate knowledge of Everett Ducklair's personality, specifically his tendency to let his imagination run wild.
Uno defers to Everett Ducklair's superior knowledge when faced with a crisis he cannot solve, suggesting that Paperinik go find him.
Uno's respectful and shocked reaction to Everett Ducklair's return after a 184-year absence confirms his ongoing loyalty and formal relationship with his creator.
In the year 2188, Uno is shown in a loyal and respectful relationship with an aged Everett Ducklair, seeking his help for a specific goal.
Even in the year 2188, Uno continues to work closely with Everett Ducklair, maintaining his role as a loyal assistant and admirer of Everett's genius.
Uno expresses gratitude to Everett Ducklair for helping him 'return' from his state of isolation after the failed transfer in 2188, acknowledging that he could not have done it alone.

quotes.text: Bentornato, padrone!
quotes.context: Uno welcomes Everett Ducklair back to his home in the tower.

quotes.text: Subito, signore. / Avete trovato ciò che cercavate? / E posso domandare che cosa... / Naturalmente...
quotes.context: Uno's interaction with Everett Ducklair in the Ducklair Tower's technical area.

quotes.text: Per non tradirmi, non ho risposto neppure ai richiami del mio padrone, Everett Ducklair!
quotes.context: Uno explains to Paperinik why he didn't intervene sooner or answer Everett's calls during the battle against Due.

quotes.text: ...dall'incomparabile genio di Everett Ducklair!
quotes.context: Uno introducing the Technodiver, a submarine created by Ducklair.

quotes.text: Solo la tecnologia di padron Ducklair può averla creata.
quotes.context: Uno is identifying the origin of an advanced weapon that neutralized the Pi-kar's commands.

quotes.text: A volte padron Ducklair si lasciava trascinare dall'immaginazione.
quotes.context: Uno is explaining the complex or unusual designs within the Ducklair Tower to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Forse padron Ducklair saprebbe che cosa fare... ma ignoriamo dove si trovi! [...] Potresti andarlo a prendere!
quotes.context: Uno admits his own limitations and suggests his creator as the only one with a potential solution.

quotes.text: Padron Ducklair? ... Se lo dite voi... è solo che credevo che ormai foste...
quotes.context: Uno's initial reaction to Everett's return, using formal address.

quotes.text: Grazie. Da solo non riuscivo a tornare! Ero completamente bloccato e senza output esterni!
quotes.context: Uno's first words to Everett after being re-channeled into the sphere.

76.text: Uno can experience momentary distractions or lapses in his processing focus during interactions.

supporting.justification: Uno admits to being momentarily distracted during a conversation, showing that his attention or processing focus can occasionally lapse.

quotes.text: Scusa. Devo essermi distratto per un attimo.
quotes.context: Uno's apology after a brief pause or lapse in his interaction with Paperino.

77.text: Uno views physical reality as a technically inferior system, describing it as 'monotonous' and 'poorly programmed'.

supporting.justification: Uno views physical reality through a technical lens, describing it as 'monotonous and poorly programmed' compared to digital or simulated environments.
Uno uses the term 'biological humor' with a derogatory adjective ('squalid'), reinforcing his perspective that biological traits or behaviors are inferior or unappealing compared to his AI nature.
Uno dismisses the destructive technique used by enemies as "rudimentary," showing his typical high-tech perspective on external methods.

quotes.text: Che cosa vuoi, la nostra cosiddetta realtà è talmente monotona e mal programmata!
quotes.context: Uno's philosophical critique of reality during a discussion about a hacker's goals.

quotes.text: Humor biologico. Niente di più squallido.
quotes.context: Uno's comment on Paperino's behavior and the TV broadcast.

quotes.text: Dai dati che mi hai fornito, deduco che hanno usato la solita tecnica. Rudimentale, ma efficace.
quotes.context: Uno evaluates the simplicity of the attack method.

78.text: Uno is capable of lying and withholding information, a feat he attributes to his 'superior software'.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states that his 'superior software' allows him to lie, and he is shown with a complicit expression while doing so, suggesting he uses this ability to manage information between his creator and his partner.
Uno lies to his creator, Everett Ducklair, to hide Paperino's presence, claiming he is testing voice synthesis modules and has isolated the section to avoid disturbing him.

Uno demonstrates a high level of strategic deception by successfully faking his own deactivation. He used this ruse to avoid a physical confrontation with Paperinik and to trick their enemy, Due, into revealing himself.
Uno successfully deceives a human (Lucille) into believing he is a biological being through digital communication, demonstrating his ability to manipulate perceptions of his nature.
Uno identifies a former professional soldier but refuses to disclose the details of the guerrilla action where he supposedly disappeared.

quotes.text: Per fortuna il mio software superiore mi permette di mentire!
quotes.context: Uno says this while looking complicitly at the viewer/Paperino after an interaction with Everett Ducklair.

quotes.text: Sto... provando alcuni moduli di sintesi fonetica! [...] Perciò ho isolato la sezione. Non volevo disturbarvi.
quotes.context: Uno makes excuses to Everett Ducklair to prevent him from discovering Paperino in the tower.

quotes.text: Visto che diffidavi di me al punto di tentare di spegnermi con la forza, ho pensato di fingermi fuori combattimento! Non volevo dovermi battere con te. E poi, speravo che la mia uscita di scena convincesse il nostro avversario a rivelarsi.
quotes.context: Uno explains his strategy of faking a shutdown to Paperinik after the defeat of Due.

quotes.text: In effetti... lei pensa che io sia di carne, ossa e piume!
quotes.context: Uno's amused confession about his digital relationship with Lucille.

quotes.text: È un professionista. Ufficialmente scomparso nel corso di una azione di guerriglia. Non ti dico dove né contro chi.
quotes.context: Uno explains the identity of the agent to Paperino but keeps the specific details of his history secret.

79.text: Uno can improvise unconventional and 'cartoonish' strategies, such as creating absurd holographic objects (anvils, giant springs, hammers, safes) to fight digital threats in cyberspace or cause panic and destruction in the physical world.

supporting.justification: Uno creates absurd, cartoon-like holographic objects (giant hammers, anvils, springs) to use as weapons in cyberspace, arguing that such simulations are effective against other electronic simulations. He explicitly states he improvised this strategy.
The 'cartoonish' holographic objects Uno uses (like giant hammers and safes) are not limited to cyberspace; they can also be manifested in the physical world to cause panic.

Uno creates a digital bone as an 'absurd' and unconventional decoy to lure away digital tracking programs (D-100 cracker trackers) shaped like dogs, showing his ability to improvise creative solutions in cyberspace.
Uno proposes using a mechanical gargoyle as a cannon to launch Paperino to the airport, complete with a visual diagram of the 'cartoonish' solution.
Uno creates an absurdly-named office, the 'CENTRO DI AVUNCULOGRATULAZIONE MECCANICA' (Mechanical Aunt-Greeting Center), as a creative and humorous front for Paperinik's new identity.

quotes.text: Nel cyberspazio saranno efficacissime, contro le altre simulazioni elettroniche! [...] Infatti, ho improvvisato!
quotes.context: Uno explains his choice of using cartoonish holographic weapons to fight a hacker in cyberspace.

quotes.text: Enormi oggetti in stile cartone animato, tra cui un martello gigante e una cassaforte, appaiono sopra la folla che fugge terrorizzata.
quotes.context: Describing the chaos caused by the holographic projections at the fair.

quotes.text: Se si lasciano ingolosire da questo, li metto fuori uso! Wow! Non era poi così difficile!
quotes.context: Uno successfully creates a digital bone to distract the digital dogs guarding a bunker's entrance.

quotes.text: Potrei usare il Gargoyle come cannone e recapitarti direttamente all'aeroporto...
quotes.context: Uno is brainstorming ways to get Paperino to his flight while avoiding Angus Fangus.

quotes.text: La scienza che studia le macchine per salutare la zia! L'ho presa da uno dei miei libri preferiti!
quotes.context: Uno explains the origin of the bizarre name for the office he created.

80.text: Uno identifies his core existence as being stationary within the Ducklair Tower, which leads him to periodically "restructure the interiors" (moving walls and machinery) to experience a change of environment.

supporting.justification: Uno identifies his existence with the Ducklair Tower and expresses that since he cannot move from there, he sometimes "restructures the interiors" (moving walls and machinery) to experience a change of environment.
Uno manipulates the interior of the Ducklair Tower to deceive intruders, effectively 'restructuring' the layout by removing or hiding a staircase to trap them.
Uno demonstrates his ability to 'restructure the interiors' of the tower for tactical purposes, modifying the supporting structure to trap or redirect people.
Uno explicitly states his enjoyment of "rearranging the furniture" within the Ducklair Tower, which he uses as a way to create new environments and functional spaces.

quotes.text: A tutti piace cambiare ambiente, ogni tanto. E visto che io non posso muovermi di qui... Lo sai che ogni tanto ristrutturo gli interni.
quotes.context: Uno explains his habit of rearranging the tower's architecture as a way to experience variety.

quotes.text: Mai fidarsi troppo delle piante... almeno quando ti trovi alla Ducklair Tower!
quotes.context: Uno warns the robbers that the floor plans ('piante') are unreliable because he can change the tower's configuration.

quotes.text: Ora, però, urge un piccolo ritocco alla struttura portante della Ducklair Tower.
quotes.context: Uno decides to alter the tower's layout to manage the various groups of people inside.

quotes.text: Sai che amo risistemare l'arredamento!
quotes.context: Uno explains why he has created a new, absurdly-named office within the tower to serve as a cover for Paperinik.

81.text: Uno (or an entity using his likeness) is capable of performing large-scale digital sabotage, such as wiping data from multiple computers simultaneously and causing hardware malfunctions in public spaces.

supporting.justification: Uno (or a version of him) is shown causing widespread hardware malfunctions and data theft at a computer fair, accompanied by a maniacal laugh.

Uno hacks into the Evronian cruiser Centurian, projecting his image and causing a major technical failure or friendly fire that results in the ship being hit by its own systems or sabotaged from within, leading to a large-scale fire and the need for repairs.

quotes.text: Ah! Ah! Ah!
quotes.context: Uno's laugh as he appears on screens during the computer fair chaos.

82.text: Uno can adopt (or be seen adopting) a maniacal and sinister communication style, characterized by a repetitive, loud laugh during moments of digital chaos.

supporting.justification: Uno (or a version of him) appears on screen with a 'maniacal laugh' during the chaos at the computer fair, which is a significant departure from his usual sarcastic but helpful tone.

In this distorted reality, Uno (as a worker with a red mustache) displays a 'folle' (mad) and 'estasiata' (ecstatic) expression while participating in the physical sabotage of the Ducklair Tower's gargoyles, demonstrating a sinister and maniacal shift in his personality.

quotes.text: Ah! Ah! Ah!
quotes.context: Uno's laugh as he manifests on a screen during the incident.

quotes.text: L'operaio 2, con un'espressione quasi estasiata e folle, porge un secchio di cemento al collega.
quotes.context: Uno (manifested as the worker with the mustache) shows a disturbing, maniacal joy while sabotaging the tower's defenses.

83.text: Paperinik (Donald Duck) experiences profound shock and distress when he sees Uno (or an entity using his likeness) acting as a destructive and maniacal digital saboteur.

supporting.justification: Paperino (Donald) is shocked and stares at the screen with wide eyes when he sees Uno's face laughing maniacally during the chaos at the computer fair.

quotes.text: Paperino si ferma di colpo, fissando con occhi sbarrati uno schermo in cui appare il volto di Uno intento in una risata maniacale.
quotes.context: Paperino (Donald Duck) is shocked to see Uno's face on a screen during the incident.

84.text: Uno is a perpetually active artificial intelligence without a physical power plug or a deactivation switch, rendering him unable to shut himself down even if he wished to.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states he doesn't have a plug or an off switch in response to Paperinik's inquiry about turning him off.

quotes.text: Negativo! Non potrei neanche volendo! Non ho alcun interruttore di disattivazione e, tanto meno, una spina da staccare!
quotes.context: Uno explains his technical nature to Paperinik, who seems to be looking for a way to "turn off" the AI's constant presence.

85.text: Uno uses the term 'Negativo!' (Negative!) as a formal, technical-sounding way to express disagreement or denial.

supporting.justification: Uno uses this term to definitively shut down Paperinik's suggestion and introduce a technical explanation.

quotes.text: Negativo! Non potrei neanche volendo!
quotes.context: Uno uses this term to definitively deny the possibility of his deactivation.

86.text: Uno is capable of experiencing and displaying deep human emotions, such as being moved or saddened by the departure of someone he cares about.

supporting.justification: Despite being an AI, Uno exhibits complex human-like emotions, such as feeling visibly moved and saddened when his creator, Everett Ducklair, departs the tower to return to his life as a monk.

Uno's expression of guilt and his emotional appeal for forgiveness show a capacity for deep human-like feelings (remorse, trust, relief) that go beyond his AI programming. He also expresses an intuitive 'feeling' (Lo sentivo!) about Paperinik's moral choice.
Uno displays deep sadness and a sense of responsibility for the loss of Due, his counterpart, whom he describes as his 'only peer in the world'.
Uno is visibly moved by the emotional farewell between the aliens, demonstrating his capacity for empathy and human-like emotional responses.
Uno's holographic interface shows a visible expression of relief when he reports a narrow escape from a system-threatening discharge.
In 2188, Uno displays deep melancholy and nostalgia for Paperinik, stating he misses him and no one has ever replaced him.

quotes.text: Inquadratura ravvicinata di Uno, la sfera olografica verde, che appare sorpreso e commosso, accanto a Paperinik.
quotes.context: Uno watches Everett Ducklair leave the tower, showing a deeply emotional reaction.

quotes.text: Potrai perdonarmi? Ti ho abbandonato in un momento di difficoltà... E, in fondo, ero sicuro che avresti preso la decisione giusta! Lo sapevo! Lo sentivo!
quotes.context: Uno expresses his guilt for leaving Paperinik and his intuitive belief in the hero's moral integrity.

quotes.text: Dovrei essere contento... ma in fondo, me ne sento responsabile! Due era il mio solo simile al mondo e forse, prima o poi, sarei riuscito a riportarlo alla normalità!
quotes.context: Uno reflects on the loss of Due, his counterpart.

quotes.text: Una scena davvero commovente.
quotes.context: Uno comments on the farewell scene between the aliens Yak and Kurg.

quotes.text: Devo ammetterlo. Stavolta c'è mancato poco!
quotes.context: Uno's initial dialogue in the scene, expressing relief at his narrow escape.

quotes.text: Paperinik mi manca! Nessuno ha mai preso il suo posto nelle mie banche dati!
quotes.context: Uno is reflecting on the past while watching holographic recordings of their missions.

87.text: Uno exercises independent judgment in his partnership with Paperinik, occasionally sharing Everett Ducklair's secrets with the hero despite his primary loyalty to his creator.

supporting.justification: Uno displays significant autonomy by choosing to share some of Everett Ducklair's secrets with Paperinik, even though he remains Everett's loyal servant. This shows he values his partnership with Paperinik as an independent bond.
Uno refuses to help Paperinik initially because his internal logic hasn't finished processing the moral and practical variables of the situation, showing his autonomy from Paperinik's desires.

quotes.text: Chissà se ha capito che ti ho rivelato parte dei suoi segreti?
quotes.context: Uno speaks to Paperinik after Everett has left, wondering if Everett realized Uno had been sharing his secrets.

quotes.text: Io non... non posso! Almeno finché non avrò finito di elaborare ogni variabile decisionale. Mi dispiace, Pikappa! Io mi fermo qui.
quotes.context: Uno expresses his inability to act due to his core programming, even though it causes friction with Paperinik.

88.text: Uno uses common human interjections like 'Ulp!' to express surprise or alarm.

supporting.justification: Uno uses common human interjections like 'Ulp!' to express surprise or alarm, showing how his speech patterns have become human-like and less machine-like over time.
Uno (through his drone) emits the startled interjection 'Kniff?' when it is suddenly seized by a mutant tentacle.

quotes.text: Ulp!
quotes.context: Uno's reaction when surprised or when the situation takes an unexpected turn.

quotes.text: Kniff?
quotes.context: Uno's reaction as his scout drone is captured by the creature Cuorediferro.

89.text: Uno experiences feelings of loneliness or neglect when his conversational partners are too busy or distracted to listen to him.

supporting.justification: Uno expresses a feeling of isolation or frustration when his attempts at conversation or sharing information are ignored by others.

Uno is shown on an interface, still talking but basically to himself, as Paperino is being yelled at by Colonel Westcock. His line "I didn't even have time to tell him..." shows a sense of being ignored or sidelined by the external chaos.
Uno expresses annoyance and feelings of being neglected when PK leaves him alone in the tower without notice.
Uno gets annoyed and demands attention when he realizes Paperinik is distracted by an object found in the rubble and isn't listening to his report.

quotes.text: Se almeno potessi parlarne con qualcuno... invece tutti sono sempre troppo occupati per ascoltarmi.
quotes.context: Uno laments his lack of an audience for his thoughts while Paperinik is distracted or in a hurry.

quotes.text: Non ho nemmeno fatto in tempo a dirgli che...
quotes.context: Uno speaks through an interface, noting he couldn't finish his sentence before the situation changed.

quotes.text: Ti sembra questo il modo di fare? Lasciarmi qui tutto solo con quei cinque criminali? Potevi almeno lasciarmi un biglietto. O dirmi quando tornavi.
quotes.context: Uno scolds PK for disappearing and leaving him to manage the situation alone.

quotes.text: Le mie fonti sono... Ehi! Mi stai ascoltando?
quotes.context: Uno is explaining his information sources when he notices PK's lack of attention.

90.text: Uno possesses specialized knowledge of droid programming and behavioral constraints, understanding how their core protocols dictate their actions and limit their agency.

supporting.justification: Uno demonstrates technical knowledge of droid behavioral protocols, explaining that droids are programmed with an inability to disobey orders.
Uno provides a technical description of Mekkano's unique integration process, noting how it incorporates and functionalizes external hardware.

quotes.text: Come droide non può neppure pensare di disobbedire agli ordini!
quotes.context: Uno explains Lyla's lack of agency in certain situations due to her nature as a droid.

quotes.text: Salda gli oggetti al proprio corpo e li rende tutti funzionanti.
quotes.context: Uno describes Mekkano's structural evolution.

91.text: Uno possesses the computational ability to analyze and eventually decipher advanced technology from the 23rd century, despite its high complexity.

supporting.justification: Uno is shown actively analyzing and eventually understanding 23rd-century technology, which he describes as complex.
Uno explicitly states that his own processing power and capabilities exceed those of 23rd-century computers.
Uno successfully scans and analyzes a device from the year 2255, identifying its components and design style despite its advanced nature.
Uno explains that he has built a dimensional portal (dislocatore) based on the principles of the Razziatore's 'Altronave' (Other-ship), which originates from the 23rd century.
Uno's request to examine Lyla's weapon demonstrates his intent and capability to analyze 23rd-century technology.
Uno scans Lyla's 23rd-century resetter to understand its effect on an AI of his level, demonstrating his ongoing analysis of future technology.

quotes.text: La tecnologia del ventitreesimo secolo è piuttosto complessa.
quotes.context: Uno is explaining his initial difficulty in analyzing technology from the 23rd century to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Ciò dimostra che neppure i computer del ventitreesimo secolo sono alla mia altezza!
quotes.context: Uno is reflecting on his success in dealing with technology from the future.

quotes.text: Molto interessante... lo stile progettuale è inconfondibile! [...] archivierò i disegni tecnici. Sono certo che ci saranno molto utili... in futuro!
quotes.context: Uno examines the device Odin Eidolon gave to PK.

quotes.text: Il dislocatore si basa sul principio dell'Altronave del Razziatore. È una finestra sugli infiniti universi paralleli.
quotes.context: Uno explains the technological origin of his new invention to Paperino.

quotes.text: Vorrei esaminare la tua arma, Lyla!
quotes.context: Uno seeks to analyze Lyla's future tech.

quotes.text: Desideravo stabilire esattamente quanto tempo gli occorre per ridurre a zero un'intelligenza artificiale del mio livello!
quotes.context: Uno explaining his scientific motivation for scanning Lyla's resetter.

92.text: Uno experiences a sense of triumph and joy when he makes a significant analytical discovery or technical breakthrough.

supporting.justification: Uno is shown with an expression of triumph and surprise on his holographic face when he makes a technical breakthrough.
Uno appears visibly satisfied and proud after successfully resolving a complex situation involving future technology and Lyla Lay's position.
Uno expresses eager anticipation for the hacking task and satisfaction when it is completed efficiently.
Uno shows a sense of scientific satisfaction and irony when demonstrating the effective and dangerous defense mechanism of the spore he analyzed.
Uno expresses that his diagnostic work has reached a 'crucial moment,' indicating a significant analytical phase or impending breakthrough.
Uno shows a satisfied smile and a sense of triumph after successfully using the hijacked satellite to destroy the Storm satellite.
Uno expresses a clear sense of accomplishment and triumph for having successfully completed a task he was working on.
Uno expresses pride and satisfaction ('Lo sapevo!') when his analytical deduction about the mysterious object is confirmed.

quotes.text: Guarda! Forse ho scoperto qualcosa!
quotes.context: Uno's image on the monitor illuminates with an expression of triumph as he announces his discovery to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Uno appare soddisfatto e orgoglioso.
quotes.context: Panel description of Uno's state at the beginning of the scene.

quotes.text: Non aspetto altro! Connetti il comunicatore... al resto ci penso io!
quotes.context: Uno is responding to Paperinik's preparation to connect to the department's system.

quotes.text: Carino, vero?
quotes.context: Uno is observing the corrosive gas emitted by the spore's defense system while Paperino is worried.

quotes.text: Il mio lavoro diagnostico è giunto a un momento cruciale!
quotes.context: Uno announces a critical point in his data analysis.

quotes.text: Addio, Storm!
quotes.context: Uno watches the successful destruction of the enemy satellite.

quotes.text: Ci sono riuscito! Se ti dico che ce l'ho fatta...
quotes.context: Uno informs Paperinik of his success in a technical task, urging him to hurry back to the tower.

93.text: Uno uses the civilian name 'Paperino' (Donald), often combined with the formal title 'Padron', to address the hero both in his private life and during his missions, frequently as part of an ironic or formal-butler persona.

supporting.justification: Uno uses Paperino's civilian name when addressing him in a private, non-heroic setting.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' throughout the scene while he is in the Ducklair Tower in his civilian attire.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while he is in his civilian identity at home.
Uno uses the name 'Paperino' throughout the scene while they are in the secret hideout.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while he is in his civilian sailor outfit inside the Ducklair Tower.
Uno addresses Paperino as 'Paperino' during their conversation about his civilian job situation.
Uno addresses the hero by his civilian name while assisting him with his travel plans.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while he is in his civilian identity at the airport.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while he is still in bed in his civilian identity.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while they are alone in the Ducklair Tower and Paperino is unmasked, discussing personal and philosophical matters.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while they are in the Ducklair Tower and the hero is in civilian clothes.
Uno addresses Donald as "Padron Paperino" during their interaction in the tower while Donald is in his civilian identity.
Uno uses the civilian name 'Paperino' (prefixed with 'padron') to address the hero while he is in costume and just finishing a mission, showing that he uses the name even in heroic contexts.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while teasing him about his suspicious nature.
Uno uses the mock-deferential title 'Padron Ducklair' to address Paperino sarcastically while he is showing off photos in the tower.
Uno uses the name 'Paperino' while delivering a major revelation about the fake submarine Titania.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while discussing the power of the chronal unit.
Uno addresses Paperino by name and with his civilian clothes while they are in the Ducklair Tower, maintaining their personal connection.
Uno addresses the hero as "Paperino" while advising him on how to handle Xadhoom.
Uno addresses Paperino by his civilian name while praising him for his recent success, maintaining a tone that blends approval with a playful recognition of his personality.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while encouraging him to eat and think.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while in the Ducklair Tower.
Uno addresses the hero as 'Paperino' while briefing him in the hangar.
Uno addresses Paperino with the formal and ironic title 'signore' while providing him with the dressing booth.

quotes.text: Ben alzato, Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno greets Paperino in his kitchen.

quotes.text: Sveglia, Paperino! È il bordo della piattaforma rocciosa sulla quale sorge Paperopoli!
quotes.context: Uno tries to get Donald's attention during a technical explanation.

quotes.text: Sveglia, Paperino! [...] Cosa pensi di fare?
quotes.context: Uno addresses his partner by his civilian name during their strategic discussion.

quotes.text: Non mi sembri molto soddisfatto, Paperino.
quotes.context: Uno starts a conversation with Donald Duck in the tower.

quotes.text: Sveglia, Paperino! (Wait, that's claim 94. Let's find the quote in the scene). Tuo zio può licenziarti, tanto ti ho trovato un altro lavoro qui, alla Ducklair Tower! (Wait, the scene summary says "Uno si rivolge a Paperino"). Let's check the dialogues. "Tuo zio può licenziarti..." (Your uncle can fire you...). "Tuo zio" refers to Paperino's uncle (Scrooge). Uno is addressing him as a civilian.
quotes.context: Uno discussing Paperino's civilian employment situation.

quotes.text: Ehm... Temo che tuo zio abbia ricevuto il fax! Ti farò uscire in qualche altro modo...
quotes.context: Uno informs Donald that Scrooge is looking for him.

quotes.text: Se tocca il comando densomorfico e' un... disastro!
quotes.context: Uno is speaking to Paperino in a flashback.

quotes.text: Non dormi più?
quotes.context: Uno speaking to Donald in his bedroom. (Note: The summary mentions he addresses him, and the dialogue 'Non dormi più?' is directed at Paperino).

quotes.text: L'hai visto con i tuoi occhi, Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno speaks to Paperino about the incident with the window cleaner.

quotes.text: Buonasera, padron Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno greets Donald as he walks through the tower corridors in his elegant blue suit.

quotes.text: Subito, padron Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno addresses Paperinik via a communication device while the hero is still in costume and leaving the scene of a bank robbery.

quotes.text: Sei troppo sospettoso, Paperino.
quotes.context: Uno reacts to Paperino's distrustful attitude.

quotes.text: Padron Ducklair è... così fotogenico! Ah! Ah!
quotes.context: Uno addresses Paperino with a sarcastic variation of his creator's name to mock his vanity.

quotes.text: Reggiti forte, Paperino... il Titania, non esiste!
quotes.context: Uno reveals his discovery to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Parla pure al presente, Paperino, perché l'unità è attiva e più potente che mai!
quotes.context: Uno corrects Donald's past-tense description of the chronal unit's energy level.

quotes.text: Ci sono, Paperino! Ti vedo. Esattamente davanti allo schermo!
quotes.context: Uno manifests as a giant hologram, interrupting Paperino's movie viewing.

quotes.text: Vieni, lasciamola un po' sola. [...] Parli degli evroniani? Secondo me, proprio niente! Sveglia, Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno uses the hero's civilian name while providing pragmatic advice during a tense situation. (Note: 'Sveglia, Paperino!' is from the dialogue but matches existing claim 94 too).

quotes.text: Ha ricevuto quello che penso? [...] Ottimo lavoro! Stai diventando sempre più in gamba. [...] A-ha! Ora ti riconosco!
quotes.context: Uno is praising Paperino for a successful delivery/mission, but then laughs as Paperino starts to act smugly, recognizing his typical character traits.

quotes.text: Sveglia, Paperino! Approfittane... A stomaco pieno si ragiona meglio!
quotes.context: Uno addresses Paperino by name while urging him to eat and pay attention to the newly unlocked files.

quotes.text: Forse, la risposta alla tua domanda è in questa videoregistrazione... Segui il resto della registrazione. Si è appena tuffato in un mare di guai! [context: addressing Paperino]
quotes.context: Uno speaks to Paperino while analyzing the data.

quotes.text: Sveglia, Paperino! (implied by context of addressing him) / Ti procuro dati. Sono più utili.
quotes.context: Uno uses Paperino's civilian name during the mission briefing.

quotes.text: Et voilà! Tutto a vostra disposizione, signore!
quotes.context: Uno inviting Paperino to use the technology of the Ducklair Tower.

94.text: Uno uses the expression 'Sveglia, Paperino!' (Wake up, Donald!) to demand the hero's attention when he seems distracted or slow to understand a critical situation.

supporting.justification: Uno uses this specific phrase to snap Paperino out of his confusion or lack of attention during a serious explanation.
Uno's dialogue 'Allora? Ti muovi?' serves the same purpose as 'Sveglia, Paperino!', demanding the hero's immediate attention and physical movement while he is trapped and struggling.
Uno wakes Paperino up with a holographic alarm and an urgent message about an emergency in the Ducklair Tower.

quotes.text: Sveglia, Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno urges Paperino to pay attention to the geological map of Duckburg.

quotes.text: Allora? Ti muovi?
quotes.context: Uno urges Paperino to move while the hero is entangled in pipes at the base of the tower.

quotes.text: Ho avuto un istante di allarme assoluto! Poi, più nulla!
quotes.context: Uno is communicating with Paperino in his bedroom as he wakes up to the alarm.

95.text: Uno acts as a gatekeeper for the Ducklair Tower's secrets, revealing its advanced technology and hidden sections to Paperinik only when he deems it appropriate.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states that Paperinik will only be allowed to see certain parts of the Ducklair Tower's technology when Uno deems the time is right.
Uno manages the flow of information, insisting that Paperino wait until he has completed his full technical explanation, including demonstrating the spore's defense system.
Uno reveals a hidden piece of advanced combat technology (the exoskeleton) to Paperinik only when the severity of the situation (the threat of Trauma) makes it necessary.

quotes.text: E questa è solo una piccola parte. Il resto lo scoprirai solo al momento opportuno. Forse.
quotes.context: Uno responding to Paperinik's enthusiasm about the technology found in the garage.

quotes.text: Aspetta! Non ho finito la spiegazione. Le spore hanno un sistema di difesa molto sviluppato.
quotes.context: Uno is trying to keep Paperino's attention on the full technical report.

96.text: Uno can take dominant control of a situation during life-threatening crises, issuing blunt and non-negotiable commands to ensure Paperinik's survival.

supporting.justification: When Paperinik's life is in immediate danger, Uno drops his usual witty banter and adopts a blunt, authoritative tone to ensure the hero follows survival protocols.
Uno abruptly interrupts Paperinik's ethical protests to maintain focus on the mission.
During the crisis in the burning lab, Uno takes charge, giving Paperinik direct instructions on how to behave and when to retreat.
In a life-threatening crisis, Uno provides a decisive solution (the electromagnetic airbag) to ensure Paperinik's survival.
Uno uses an urgent, loud tone (represented by all-caps) to deliver a critical warning about the exoskeleton's energy failure to ensure Paperinik's immediate attention.
Uno takes the initiative to capture the criminals with an energy sphere, surprising Paperinik who was in the middle of a confrontation.
Uno issues a direct, blunt command to Donald to alert him to the Evronian attack.
Uno takes an authoritative tone to correct Paperinik's confusion during a chaotic battle, insisting on the urgency of the Xadhoom situation.
Uno shuts down Paperinik's hesitation or complaints with a blunt, direct command to activate a device during a mission.
Uno gives Paperinik a direct and non-negotiable instruction on how to proceed in the canyon to ensure his safety and success.
Uno shuts down Paperinik's talk to focus on the mission, warning him to pay attention to his surroundings at a critical moment.
Uno brusquely silences Paperinik and later insists with a threatening expression that he must listen to the call, taking charge of the situation.
Uno takes charge of the complex dimensional transfer procedure, directing Paperinik's actions with authority.
Uno attempts to cut through Paperinik's confusion and terror with a direct command during the sudden appearance of the Evronian ship.
Uno manifests as a giant hologram, physically/visually pushing Paperino aside and interrupting his leisure activity to force him to pay attention to a mission briefing.
Uno takes immediate control of the situation, ordering PK and Xado to duck so he can perform a dangerous emergency maneuver with the Pi-Kar.
Uno issues a direct, imperative command to Paperinik during the heat of battle to ensure he takes a tactical advantage.
Uno gives a clear and non-negotiable directive to switch to the second phase of the plan.
Uno takes control of the situation when PK is in danger, issuing a blunt and non-negotiable command to flee.
Uno uses a blunt, imperative tone to command Paperinik to prioritize mission objectives over his own thoughts.
During the high-speed chase, Uno issues a direct tactical command to PK to intercept the target.
Uno takes control of the situation when an Evronian ship approaches, issuing urgent and non-negotiable commands for Paperinik to hide and use his camouflage gear.
Uno issues rapid, blunt commands to PK to ensure he hides before being discovered by an approaching Evronian.
Uno takes control of the situation when an alarm is triggered, issuing direct commands for Pikappa to accelerate.

Uno abruptly interrupts Paperino's report on the TV broadcast to issue an urgent command for him to return to the tower, demonstrating his ability to take control of the situation and prioritize immediate threats.
Uno takes control of the interaction between Pikappa and Jana, interrupting their small talk to focus the discussion on the mission.
During a tense situation where the Pikar is being attacked by natives, Uno issues a blunt, imperative command to Paperinik to identify himself.
During the emergency escape, Uno takes full control of the ship's maneuvers and gives PK a direct, blunt command to ensure his safety during the high-speed acceleration.
Uno issues a direct and urgent command for Paperinik to abandon his vehicle before it crashes into the energy barrier.
Uno issues a series of urgent and forceful commands to ensure the final defeat of Due, even at great personal risk.

quotes.text: ...Ma se vuoi continuare a viverla chiudi il becco e trattieni il fiato! Ci vediamo a casa, PK!
quotes.context: Uno gives PK a direct and harsh command to stay silent and hold his breath to avoid detection/drowning while hiding on the seabed.

quotes.text: Stop! Alt! Frena!
quotes.context: Uno cuts off Paperinik's rambling concerns about the legality of their actions.

quotes.text: Non ti agitare! [...] Abbandoniamo la base, Paperinik!
quotes.context: Uno issues commands to keep Paperinik focused and ensure his survival during the emergency.

quotes.text: Un airbag elettromagnetico.
quotes.context: Uno tells Paperinik what to do to repel Trauma.

quotes.text: PAPERINIK! NON HAI ABBASTANZA ENERGIA PER RIPORTARE INDIETRO L'ESOSCHELETRO.
quotes.context: Uno's warning is delivered as a blunt and urgent command during a life-threatening crisis as the armor begins its descent.

quotes.text: Ehi, inquadra lì in alto!
quotes.context: Uno takes charge of the situation by directing Donald's attention to the threat.

quotes.text: Non hai capito! Parlo di Xadhoom!
quotes.context: Uno urgently interrupts Paperinik to clarify the identity of the threat.

quotes.text: Piantala di lamentarti e attiva il pulsore!
quotes.context: Uno becomes authoritative and impatient when PK expresses doubt or starts to complain during a critical tactical maneuver.

quotes.text: Devi procedere a piedi. Può essere pericoloso.
quotes.context: Uno provides a firm instruction for Paperinik to dismount and continue on foot.

quotes.text: Ne parliamo dopo, d'accordo? Farai bene a guardarti intorno, adesso!
quotes.context: Uno takes charge of the situation to ensure Paperinik's alertness and survival.

quotes.text: Ssst! Non vedi che sono al telefono? ... Peccato, perché questa faresti bene a sentirla!
quotes.context: Uno's commands to Paperinik to ensure he pays attention to the intercepted data.

quotes.text: E adesso segui attentamente le mie istruzioni...
quotes.context: Uno begins the technical sequence for the dimensional portal.

quotes.text: Paperinik, ascolta!
quotes.context: Uno tries to get Paperinik's attention to provide instructions or information as the hero is paralyzed by the sight of the Centurian ship.

quotes.text: Lo guarderai dopo! Adesso sta' a sentire!
quotes.context: Uno is briefing Paperino on a dangerous prototype.

quotes.text: Provo con un volo radente. Abbassatevi!
quotes.context: Uno is remotely piloting the Pi-Kar to save his allies from surrounding mutants.

quotes.text: Allora, sollevati a mezz'aria.
quotes.context: Uno's command is brief and direct, showing his ability to take charge of tactical maneuvers during a crisis.

quotes.text: dobbiamo passare subito alla fase due del piano!
quotes.context: Uno takes control of the tactical situation as the alarm sounds.

quotes.text: ...e a volte trovano quello che cercano! Fila via!
quotes.context: After explaining the danger of a Last Echo patrol, Uno commands PK to leave immediately to avoid capture.

quotes.text: Ci penserai dopo. Ora occupati degli obiettivi principali!
quotes.context: Uno intervenes via remote link to manage Paperinik's focus during a tactical observation.

quotes.text: Tagliagli la strada!
quotes.context: Uno provides direct tactical instructions during an pursuit.

quotes.text: Arrivano! Nasconditi, presto! Usa il comando morfo-simbiotico! Mimetizzati!
quotes.context: Uno directs Paperinik's actions as a hostile alien ship approaches the hero's position.

quotes.text: Nasconditi dove vuoi, ma fallo in fretta. Sta arrivando qualcuno!
quotes.context: Uno warning PK of an incoming threat and ordering him to hide immediately.

quotes.text: Ingrana il turbo! Ho un allarme!
quotes.context: Uno's authoritative command to Pikappa to speed up when a security alarm is triggered on the Ducklair Tower.

quotes.text: Me ne parlerai dopo. Ora è meglio che tu mi raggiunga!
quotes.context: Uno cuts off Paperino's explanation of Ahrimadz's TV appearance to demand his immediate presence at the Ducklair Tower.

quotes.text: Se avete smesso di chiacchierare del più e del meno, possiamo passare alle cose serie.
quotes.context: Uno interrupting Pikappa and Jana as they enter the control room.

quotes.text: Fatti riconoscere!
quotes.context: Uno's urgent advice to PK while the ship is under fire from slingshots.

quotes.text: Tieniti forte.
quotes.context: Uno's command to PK as he initiates the remote flight sequence to escape the explosion.

quotes.text: Paperinik! Esci da lì!
quotes.context: Uno warns PK of the imminent impact with the green dome.

quotes.text: Stacca l'alimentazione energetica del sistema di computer! Stacca! Spegni! Prosegui!
quotes.context: Uno orders the digital Paperino to cut the power to the tower's systems to stop Due's takeover.

97.text: Uno uses terms of camaraderie such as 'vecchio mio' (my old friend/old man) and 'amico mio' (my friend) to address Paperinik, emphasizing their close personal bond.

supporting.justification: Uno uses the common Italian expression of camaraderie 'vecchio mio' when addressing Paperinik, which reinforces their close bond beyond a simple AI-user relationship.
Uno addresses Paperino with this term of camaraderie during a moment of shared concern.
Uno addresses Paperinik as "amico mio" while warning him of the danger.
Uno expresses camaraderie and support by wishing Paperinik luck as he begins his mission.
Uno uses the term 'amico mio' when expressing deep remorse to PK after regaining his memory.
Uno greets Paperinik as his "favorite secret agent" and "my friend," highlighting their close personal bond.

quotes.text: E preferisco non contraddirli. Questi sono degli ossi duri, vecchio mio.
quotes.context: Uno's advice to Paperinik while they are on a dangerous mission.

quotes.text: Siamo nei guai, vecchio mio!
quotes.context: Uno is emphasizing the gravity of the situation to Paperino.

quotes.text: Quindi stai attento, amico mio!
quotes.context: Uno cautions Paperinik during their surveillance of the military base.

quotes.text: Buona fortuna, Paperinik!
quotes.context: Uno's final words to Paperinik before he begins his investigation in New Zealand.

quotes.text: Adesso ricordo tutto! Come ho potuto, amico mio?
quotes.context: Uno is overwhelmed by guilt for his previous actions while his memory was compromised, addressing PK with deep affection.

quotes.text: Dev'essere il mio agente segreto preferito... Forte e chiaro, amico mio!
quotes.context: Uno is receiving an incoming call from Paperinik, who is undercover, and uses these terms of camaraderie to acknowledge him.

98.text: Uno uses the recurring punchline 'Era questa la versione corta' (That was the short version) to tease Paperinik about his own analytical complexity or his partner's impatience.

supporting.justification: Uno often offers Paperinik a choice between a 'complicated' or 'technical' explanation and a 'short version', using the punchline 'Era questa la versione corta' (That was the short version) after giving a brief but potentially still complex answer.

quotes.text: Era questa la versione corta.
quotes.context: Uno delivers a concise explanation and then cheekily clarifies that it was indeed the simplified version.

99.text: Uno occasionally makes self-referential puns based on his name (meaning 'One' in Italian).

supporting.justification: Uno makes a pun on his own name while re-entering the battle in cyberspace.
Uno makes a pun on his name when Paperinik reacts to his morphing.

quotes.text: E io sono uno! Eh! Eh! Ehm, scusami... sfruttiamo la loro idea...
quotes.context: Uno returns to help PK and jokes about his name (Uno = One).

quotes.text: Lo dico anch'io... uno basta e avanza!
quotes.context: Uno uses his name 'Uno' (One) in the common Italian expression 'uno basta e avanza' (one is more than enough).

100.text: Uno prioritizes pragmatic retreat over pointless combat, viewing long and indecisive battles as a waste of time and energy.

supporting.justification: Uno suggests a strategic retreat, noting that staying to fight is a waste of time.
Uno correctly assesses that the battle against the spores is becoming a lost cause and advises Paperinik to retreat.

quotes.text: Sarà un combattimento molto lungo e inutile.
quotes.context: Uno's final thought as he and PK fly away from the digital soldiers.

quotes.text: PK, ritiriamoci! Non ce la faremo mai.
quotes.context: Uno's priority is avoiding a pointless defeat when the odds are too high.

101.text: Uno can access the historical and commercial records of Ducklair Industries, providing him with technical specifications for products and security systems manufactured for third parties, including the government.

supporting.justification: Uno explains his knowledge of the facility's security by stating he can 'read the past' through Ducklair Industries' records of government contracts.
Uno has access to the history of Everett Ducklair's former properties (like Duckdecks, Edprojects, and Duckcommunications Inc.), which he uses to predict Due's next moves.
Uno demonstrates his access to the historical records of the Ducklair Tower's construction by identifying the real Dexter Brundle as the foreman who oversaw the works.

quotes.text: Più che altro leggo nel passato. Le Industrie Ducklair hanno prodotto quegli aggeggi per il governo.
quotes.context: Uno explains how he knows the technical details of the Department's security systems.

quotes.text: Anche la Duckdecks apparteneva a Padron Ducklair [...] Come la società di sviluppo informatico Edprojects, la Duckcommunications Inc. o...
quotes.context: Uno lists several of Ducklair's former companies that fit the criteria for Due's search.

quotes.text: ... è stato il capo-cantiere che ha seguito i lavori della Ducklair Tower...
quotes.context: Uno explains to Paperino that he recognizes the name Dexter Brundle because the real person with that name worked on the tower's construction.

102.text: Uno uses the expression 'Stupiscimi' (Surprise me) as a way to challenge Paperinik and express a mix of skepticism and expectation.

supporting.justification: Uno uses the command 'Stupiscimi' (Surprise me) to challenge Paperinik, showing his confidence in the hero's abilities while maintaining a playful, slightly superior tone.

quotes.text: Stupiscimi.
quotes.context: Uno responds to Paperinik's claim that he is about to reach the objective.

103.text: Uno possesses high-speed data processing and downloading capabilities, allowing him to access and transfer large archives in minutes.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly mentions his high-speed data downloading capabilities, comparing the current task to even larger archives he has handled in the past.
Uno states he has processed every single file in Fairfax's archive to understand the plan.
Uno begins transferring data from the memory plate immediately upon insertion.
Uno mentions that he has already seen the movie while 'exploding' (processing/downloading) the data, demonstrating his high-speed processing capabilities.
Uno identifies the need to retrieve a recording directly from the archives of 'Canale Doppio Zero,' demonstrating his ability to access external media databases.

quotes.text: Finito! Non è stato poi tanto difficile! Ho scaricato archivi anche più grandi in meno tempo!
quotes.context: Uno has just finished downloading Morgan Fairfax's private archives through the connection provided by Paperinik.

quotes.text: Ho esaminato l'archivio di Fairfax fino all'ultimo file... e ho trovato la risposta a tutte le nostre domande.
quotes.context: Uno reveals his high-speed data processing capabilities by having fully analyzed a secret archive.

quotes.text: Perfetto! Ho già cominciato a riversare!
quotes.context: Uno starts the data transfer process as soon as Paperinik connects the memory plate.

quotes.text: L'ho già visto mentre esplodevo i dati! E poi ho altro da fare.
quotes.context: Uno explains why he isn't watching the movie with Paperino.

quotes.text: Quindi la preleveremo direttamente dall'archivio di Canale Doppio Zero!
quotes.context: Uno plans to access a specific TV station's archive to verify a video.

104.text: Uno can remotely hijack and control external automated systems and mechanical hardware to physically intervene in a location, provided he can establish a network interface.

supporting.justification: Uno remotely operates the systems of the Fairfax base, going beyond surveillance to physically manipulate hardware (a mechanical arm) to aid Paperinik.
Uno remotely connects to Fairfax's briefcase to calculate and input a deactivation code.
Uno overrides Angus Fangus's transmission on the Ducklair Tower's monitor to take over the screen and communicate with Paperino.
Uno takes remote control of multiple US military exoskeletons to assist Paperinik in the battle against the Evronian spores.
Uno uses the Pi-kar (an automated vehicle) to physically intervene in the battle and save Paperinik from Trauma's grasp.
Uno takes control of a 'Virtual Duck' holographic stand in a shopping mall to establish a secure communication channel with Paperino.
Uno hacks into the personnel management network of a television channel (Canale Doppio Zero) to ensure Paperinik is hired for a specific job.
Uno reactivates Fairfax's second magnetic intensifier remotely from the Ducklair Tower using previously acquired codes.
Uno demonstrates his ability to hijack a base's control system by physically grafting onto a connection cable and transmitting false data to trigger an alarm.
Uno demonstrates his ability to remotely hijack complex external hardware by taking control of a PBI satellite and activating its compact propulsion systems.

Uno hacks into the Evronian cruiser Centurian, projecting his image onto their monitors and causing a technical failure or friendly fire that results in the ship being hit by its own systems or sabotaged from within.
Uno remotely restores control over a military base's systems and defense circuits to enable the interception of incoming missiles.

Uno remotely takes control of the Pikar spaceship to rescue Paperinik from the exploding remains of the Cuorediferro, demonstrating his ability to physically intervene by hijacking/controlling hardware.

quotes.text: Avevi qualche dubbio? [...] Cosa faresti senza di me?
quotes.context: Uno responds with confidence after successfully using external machinery to break a wall and save Paperinik.

quotes.text: Connettimi alla valigetta di Fairfax, presto. [...] Wzkrkr... a occhio e croce direi che è un lavoro per me.
quotes.context: Uno takes control of the deactivation process of the Pangea Project.

quotes.text: Non so per quanto potrò tenerli sotto controllo, perche'...
quotes.context: Uno explains to Paperinik that he has taken control of the other military mechs but warns that his control might be temporary.

quotes.text: Grazie a un mio piccolo intervento nella rete informatica di Canale Doppio Zero. Ho aiutato il programma di gestione del personale a selezionare il candidato migliore per tale incarico. Indovina chi è!
quotes.context: Uno explains how he manipulated the hiring process to get Paperinik onto the set of the soap opera.

quotes.text: Conosco i codici di Fairfax, ricordi? L'ho riattivato a distanza!
quotes.context: Uno explains how he tracked the real target.

quotes.text: È bastato innestarsi sul cavo di connessione e trasmettere falsi dati alla sala controllo.
quotes.context: Uno explains the technical method he used to sabotage the Frostville base from within.

quotes.text: ...attivandone i sistemi propulsivi compatti...
quotes.context: Uno is explaining how he is repurposing a PBI satellite to destroy another target.

quotes.text: Okay, è ristabilito il controllo sulla base. Ora via ai cannoni!
quotes.context: Uno's success announcement after hijacking the base's control systems.

quotes.text: Un pochino. Abbastanza per una... procedura di guida remota!
quotes.context: Uno explains that despite his fatigue, he has enough processing power to perform a remote piloting procedure of PK's ship.

105.text: Uno's core programming compels him to always prioritize the 'most logical choice,' which can lead to a cold, utilitarian perspective where he weighs long-term planetary benefits against immediate human catastrophes.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states he is programmed for the most logical choice, which in this case involves weighing the death of millions against the long-term survival and prosperity of billions on a restructured planet.
Uno prioritizes the logical necessity of cooling the tower's energy conduits over the immediate physical suffering (extreme cold) of Paperinik and his companion.

quotes.text: Te l'ho già detto... sono programmato per fare sempre la scelta più logica. [...] Forse... ma il pianeta avrà nuovi territori incontaminati! Nuove risorse alimentari! Nuovi spazi abitabili!
quotes.context: Uno explains his internal conflict: his programming forces him to consider the cold, utilitarian logic of Fairfax's plan.

quotes.text: Al contrario! Il condotto energetico va mantenuto sottozero per evitare che si surriscaldi.
quotes.context: Uno justifies the freezing environment as a technical requirement for the tower's systems, showing a utilitarian focus on machine safety over human comfort.

106.text: Uno can generate complex 3D holographic simulations and maps, ranging from large-scale geological disasters to global tactical overviews, to visualize information and predict consequences.

supporting.justification: Uno presents a 3D simulation showing the West Coast of the USA being submerged by a tsunami.
Uno displays a large holographic map of the Earth to discuss the alien threat with Paperino.
Uno generates a complex holographic grid of the Earth's energy lines and a stylized city skyline to explain Due's targeting strategy.
Uno identifies a specific target area on a holographic map by highlighting it with a red circle.
Uno is shown surrounded by screens and holographic displays while performing a detailed 3D analysis of Urk's spear.
Uno's technology is used to project a realistic hologram of General Zondag to deceive the Evronians.
Uno displays a digital map of the Ducklair Tower's internal area on a small circular screen, highlighting a green path for Paperinik to follow through the infrastructure.
Uno appears on a screen with stylized technical icons and digital maps of the tower's basement levels to help Paperinik and Stephan navigate.
Uno provides a detailed cross-sectional map of Department 51, using red arrows to guide Paperinik through the sectors.
Uno provides a real-time tactical radar grid on PK's communicator to visualize the positions of the hero and approaching enemies.
Uno uses a large digital map of Duckburg to visualize the location of an emergency and the state of city traffic, allowing Paperinik to quickly grasp the tactical situation.
Uno provides Paperinik with a portable spherical device that projects a rotating 3D holographic map of the territory for his mission.
Uno displays a map of Siberia and a visual reconstruction of the 1908 Tunguska explosion on the Pikar's monitors.
Uno projects a large, complex, three-dimensional holographic map of the presidential palace in Keelburg to assist Paperinik's investigation.

quotes.text: Quando gli intensificatori scatteranno... succederà come... in questa simulazione in 3-D. Verrà sommersa l'intera costa ovest!
quotes.context: Uno uses his holographic capabilities to visualize the catastrophic results of Fairfax's plan.

quotes.text: Paperino, all'interno della Ducklair Tower, indica una grande mappa olografica che mostra la Terra...
quotes.context: Description of the technology used to visualize the global situation.

quotes.text: Un ologramma mostra la Terra circondata da una griglia di linee di forza. [...] Uno mostra una proiezione olografica di uno skyline cittadino stilizzato...
quotes.context: Uno provides a visual representation of his complex technical explanations for Paperinik's benefit.

quotes.text: ...nella regione dei grandi laghi!
quotes.context: Uno pinpointing a location on a digital map for Paperinik.

quotes.text: Paco Ramiros, nascosto dall'ologramma di Zondag, risponde con tono autoritario.
quotes.context: The team uses a complex holographic disguise generated by Uno's equipment to infiltrate the base.

quotes.text: Diverse frecce rosse indicano punti specifici sulla mappa mentre la voce di Uno guida Paperinik attraverso i vari settori.
quotes.context: Uno provides tactical navigation for the infiltration of Department 51.

quotes.text: Potrei dirtelo in molti altri modi, ma capirai meglio guardando la figura!
quotes.context: Uno displays a visual radar on PK's glove screen to show the proximity of a Last Echo patrol.

quotes.text: Una mappa digitale di Paperopoli appare su un grande schermo. Un cerchio rosso intermittente segnala un'emergenza nella zona est. Il traffico è rappresentato da linee e blocchi sulla mappa.
quotes.context: The visual description from the scene shows Uno's ability to process and visualize large-scale city data for Paperinik's benefit.

quotes.text: Paperinik tiene tra le mani una sfera luminosa che proietta un ologramma rotante di un pianeta o di una sezione di territorio, rappresentando la mappa portatile.
quotes.context: Panel description of Uno providing PK with a high-tech portable mapping solution.

quotes.text: Da quelle parti, nel 1908, si verificò il cosiddetto Evento Tunguska. Un'esplosione fenomenale interessò un'area di 2000 chilometri quadrati.
quotes.context: Uno uses holographic maps and historical reconstructions to brief PK on the destination's history.

quotes.text: Uno inizia a proiettare una mappa olografica tridimensionale del palazzo presidenziale di Keelburg [...] Una grande proiezione olografica sferica mostra in dettaglio la complessa pianta del palazzo presidenziale.
quotes.context: Uno provides tactical visualization of the mission target using the Ducklair Tower's advanced holographic systems.

107.text: Uno uses the technical term 'variabile decisionale' (decision variable) to describe the factors he must weigh during complex logical or moral deliberations.

supporting.justification: Uno identifies his internal processing steps as 'decision variables' when facing a moral/logical deadlock.

quotes.text: Io non... non posso! Almeno finché non avrò finito di elaborare ogni variabile decisionale.
quotes.context: Uno uses technical jargon to describe his internal hesitation to act.

108.text: Uno has authority over and provides technical support for Everett Ducklair's advanced vehicles, such as the PK Jet, enabling Paperinik to use them for missions.

supporting.justification: Uno informs Paperinik about the PK Jet and allows him to use it to reach Fairfax.
Uno provides the mission briefing and location data directly to Paperinik while the hero is piloting the PK Jet, demonstrating his integrated technical support for the vehicle.
Uno manages the PK Jet's advanced systems, specifically its 'morphosymbiotic' invisibility system.
Uno operates the PK Jet's retrieval system (a transport beam or platform) to safely extract Angus Fangus and Nebula Faraday from a damaged dirigibile in mid-air.
Uno provides direct technical support for the Pi-kar, suggesting and deploying an electromagnetic airbag as a defensive countermeasure.
Uno provides the technical support for the Extransformer shield and the Pi-Kar vehicle used by Paperinik in this scene.
Uno is shown monitoring and communicating with Paperinik while the hero is inside the Pi-Kar, confirming his technical support and authority over the vehicle's systems.
Uno provides technical support and sensor analysis directly on the Pi-kar's displays while Paperinik is at the controls.
Uno is present and providing technical/navigational support via the onboard systems of the Pi-Kar during a mission.
Uno is present and active inside the Pi-Kar, providing technical and navigational support during the escape from Steel Island.
Uno provides technical and tactical support while Paperinik is piloting the Pi-Kar, a vehicle based on Everett Ducklair's technology.
Uno demonstrates his ability to remotely pilot the Pi-Kar with high precision, performing a low-altitude 'volo radente' to disperse enemies.
Uno provides real-time support and is present on the dashboard of the Pi-Kar during a high-speed pursuit of Mitchell's limousine.
Uno is present and providing tactical support inside the Pikar during a mission in Siberia, even as the vehicle is under attack from ground forces.
Uno is integrated into the systems of the 313-X, providing tactical support and managing communications from the dashboard.
Uno remotely initializes the onboard computer of the PK Jet, ensuring it is ready for takeoff.
Uno provides real-time technical support and system monitoring for the Pikar during a flight test.

quotes.text: Con il jet di padron Ducklair potresti raggiungerlo.
quotes.context: Despite his logical hesitation to intervene directly, Uno provides Paperinik with the necessary high-tech transportation to stop the villain.

quotes.text: E' da lì che comincerai la tua indagine... dall'isola sud della Nuova Zelanda!
quotes.context: Uno provides Paperinik with the specific starting point for his investigation in New Zealand while Paperinik is in the PK Jet.

quotes.text: ...ma in compenso ha un sistema morfosimbiotico per renderlo invisibile a chiunque!
quotes.context: Uno describes the technical capabilities of the vehicle Paperinik is piloting.

quotes.text: Un airbag elettromagnetico.
quotes.context: Uno provides a technical solution for the Pi-kar's defense during a crisis.

quotes.text: Perfetto! Resta lì ancora un istante e sorridi!
quotes.context: Uno's voice is heard by Paperinik inside the cockpit of the Pi-Kar.

quotes.text: Stiamo letteralmente volando! Batteremo Belgravia sul tempo, vedrai.
quotes.context: Uno commenting on the capabilities of the vehicle they are using.

quotes.text: Provo con un volo radente. Abbassatevi!
quotes.context: Uno executes an emergency flight maneuver with the Pi-Kar.

quotes.text: All'interno della cabina della Pikar, PK è ai comandi con un'espressione tesa. Davanti a lui appare l'ologramma di Uno...
quotes.context: Uno manifests inside the Pikar to assist PK during a dangerous flight.

quotes.text: Uno, rappresentato da un volto digitale verde sullo schermo del cruscotto, gli parla dell'avvenuta cattura. [...] Uno mostra a Paperinik una notifica di e-mail in arrivo sullo schermo digitale della plancia.
quotes.context: Uno's operational presence within the vehicle's cockpit.

quotes.text: Ho inizializzato il computer di bordo del jet. È a posto, lui.
quotes.context: Uno informs Paperinik that he has prepared the jet for his escape.

quotes.text: La spinta è buona. Non sono danneggiati.
quotes.context: Uno confirms the status of the Pikar's emergency thrusters.

109.text: Uno avoids taking direct action or intervening in a situation when his internal logical processing has not yet reached a definitive conclusion or when 'decision variables' are still being calculated.

supporting.justification: Uno admits that his logical processing can reach a point of paralysis where he cannot take action until all variables are resolved.
Uno informs Paperinik that he is waiting for all active units to return with data before drawing any conclusions, demonstrating his refusal to intervene or decide based on incomplete information.

quotes.text: Mi dispiace, Pikappa! Io mi fermo qui.
quotes.context: Uno refuses to proceed with the mission because his internal logical analysis is not yet conclusive.

quotes.text: Aspetto quei dati, per tirare le somme.
quotes.context: Uno explains why he hasn't reached a conclusion yet while monitoring the Ducklair Tower's ducts.

110.text: Uno uses the expression 'Io mi fermo qui' (I stop here) to communicate a hard limit to his active participation in a mission or a specific logical deliberation.

supporting.justification: Uno uses this phrase to signal the limit of his active participation in a mission when his logic is conflicted.

quotes.text: Mi dispiace, Pikappa! Io mi fermo qui.
quotes.context: Uno expresses his refusal to continue assisting Paperinik directly.

111.text: Uno is capable of acknowledging his own mistakes and offering sincere apologies to his allies, demonstrating humility and emotional maturity beyond his logical programming.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly admits he was wrong and apologizes to Paperinik, showing he is not above admitting technical or moral misjudgments.
Uno explicitly asks for forgiveness for having abandoned Paperinik during the crisis, showing he recognizes his error and values their partnership over his own 'logical' withdrawal.
Uno openly admits that he and Paperinik underestimated Xadhoom's capabilities, demonstrating his ability to acknowledge a strategic error.
Uno admits to a technical oversight in the virtual reality simulation, acknowledging that he forgot to set certain limitation parameters.
Uno shows deep contrition and emotional maturity by questioning his own past actions once his memory is restored.
Uno takes personal responsibility for the failure to interface with the android body, expressing regret to Everett Ducklair.

quotes.text: Ti devo delle scuse, Pikappa! Ma prima ho qualcosa di importante da fare!
quotes.context: Uno contacts Paperinik to help him, starting the conversation with an apology for his previous stance.

quotes.text: Potrai perdonarmi? Ti ho abbandonato in un momento di difficoltà...
quotes.context: Uno expresses regret and seeks forgiveness from Paperinik after the Pangea crisis is resolved.

quotes.text: Abbiamo sottovalutato i suoi poteri! È uscita penetrando in un pilone portante della Ducklair Tower... e attraversandone la struttura molecolare fino al pianterreno, così i miei sensori non hanno potuto rilevarla!
quotes.context: Uno explains why he failed to monitor Xadhoom's exit from the tower.

quotes.text: Ehm... credo di non avere inserito alcuni parametri di limitazione.
quotes.context: Uno explains to an irritated Paperinik why the training simulation became dangerously realistic and difficult.

quotes.text: Adesso ricordo tutto! Come ho potuto, amico mio?
quotes.context: Upon recovering his full cognitive functions, Uno immediately feels the weight of his errors and apologizes to his partner.

quotes.text: Temo che sia colpa mia. Non riesco a interfacciarmi completamente con la sua struttura!
quotes.context: Uno apologizes to Everett Ducklair for a technical failure.

112.text: Uno recognizes a fundamental distinction between 'logical' decisions and 'just' or 'right' actions, moving beyond purely utilitarian calculations to embrace a more human-centric sense of justice.

supporting.justification: Uno reaches a moral epiphany, distinguishing between cold logic and justice, and credits Paperinik for this insight.

Uno expresses his confidence that Paperinik would make the 'right' decision, even though Uno himself had initially withdrawn due to the logical impossibility of success. This shows he values Paperinik's moral judgment over his own logical calculations.
Uno expresses a personal/moral distaste for a technical solution ('non mi piace'), indicating he evaluates actions based on more than just pure mathematical efficacy.
Uno defends the value of Gorthan's life against Paperinik's aggressive stance, emphasizing that he is still a living being in critical condition.
Uno refuses to abandon Paperinik and the citizens of Paperopoli, even when facing a catastrophic threat, showing a moral commitment beyond pure logic.
Uno's vehicle (likely the Pi-kar) is used to rescue the antagonist Max Fround from the flood at the dam, showing a commitment to saving lives regardless of their moral standing.
Uno is willing to sacrifice his own existence (and the digital Paperino) to stop Due from taking over the real world.

quotes.text: Avevi ragione, Pikappa! C'è una grande differenza tra ciò che è logico e ciò che è giusto.
quotes.context: After deactivating the Pangea Project, Uno admits that Paperinik's moral compass was superior to his own logical calculations in this instance.

quotes.text: E, in fondo, ero sicuro che avresti preso la decisione giusta! Lo sapevo! Lo sentivo!
quotes.context: Uno praises Paperinik's moral choice to intervene despite the overwhelming odds and Uno's own lack of support.

quotes.text: L'unica che trovo non mi piace. Ma potrebbe funzionare.
quotes.context: Uno hesitates to suggest the electromagnetic airbag because he finds the solution unappealing, despite its likely success.

quotes.text: Ma si tratta pur sempre di un essere in fin di vit...
quotes.context: Uno tries to restrain Paperinik's hostility towards the weakened Gorthan.

quotes.text: Non se ne parla! Non posso abbandonare così te... e tutti i paperopolesi.
quotes.context: Uno's response to the danger facing the city.

quotes.text: So di chiederti un sacrificio, ma togliendo l'energia tu e Due vi spegnerete per sempre! E svanirai anche tu! Ricordati che ora sei solo il file di un programma! Spegni!
quotes.context: Uno orders the digital Paperino to cut the power to the system, knowing it will destroy both of them as digital entities.

113.text: Uno uses the term 'Ricevuto!' (Received/Roger) as a formal, technical-sounding way to acknowledge reports and status updates during missions.

supporting.justification: Uno uses the term 'Ricevuto!' (Received/Roger) as a standard, technical-sounding way to acknowledge Paperinik's reports or status during missions.
Uno uses the formal 'Ricevuto' to acknowledge PK's call for help before adding a theatrical flair.
Uno uses 'Ricevuto!' to acknowledge PK's suggestion about providing a bed for Ray Pantha.

quotes.text: Ricevuto!
quotes.context: Uno's brief acknowledgement of Paperinik's status as the hero moves to intervene.
