quotes.text: Ricevuto. Arriva la cavalleria.
quotes.context: Uno responds to PK's request for assistance during the battle.

quotes.text: Ricevuto! Dovrei averne una abbastanza dura in magazzino!
quotes.context: Uno confirms he has received the request/suggestion and will act on it.

114.text: Uno's hardware and software architecture is designed for extreme longevity, with a projected operational lifespan exceeding three hundred years.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states that his configuration is designed to last for more than three centuries.
In the year 2188, Uno is still fully operational and overseeing the city of Duckburg, confirming his extreme longevity.
In the year 2188, Uno is shown still fully operational and interacting with Everett Ducklair.
Uno is shown to be active and operational in the year 2188, nearly two centuries after his initial partnership with Paperinik, confirming his extreme operational longevity.
Uno is shown to be active and operational in the year 2188, working alongside Everett Ducklair in a laboratory.
Uno's continued operation and management of the Ducklair Tower in 2188 confirms his extreme longevity and operational stability over centuries.

quotes.text: La mia configurazione è progettata per durare ben più di trecento anni.
quotes.context: Uno is explaining why he could theoretically still be active in the 23rd century.

quotes.text: Impenetrabile e totalmente schermato! Non so come aiutarti a rintracciarlo.
quotes.context: Uno is providing operational support in a futuristic Duckburg.

quotes.text: Ritorno nel futuro. [...] Sullo schermo appare il volto stilizzato e verde di Uno.
quotes.context: The scene is explicitly set in the future (2188), where Uno is still active.

115.text: Uno's future evolution is the character Odin Eidolon, a droid controlled by an artificial intelligence that remains active into the 23rd century.

supporting.justification: Uno reveals to the reader (and himself) that he will eventually become the 23rd-century droid Odin Eidolon.
Uno confirms he is in contact with Odin Eidolon, who sent him a recording of the events Paperinik experienced.

In the year 2188, Uno is present as a green hologram while Everett Ducklair completes the assembly of a complex robotic body, which is the precursor to Uno's future physical evolution as Odin Eidolon.
Uno's shadow on the wall, as he reflects on his new body, clearly projects the silhouette of his future form, Odin Eidolon, showing his long-term planning for his own evolution.

quotes.text: Odin Eidolon è... anzi, sarà un droide. Un droide guidato da un'intelligenza artificiale che nel 2255 sarà antica quanto quella dell'ultimo evroniano!
quotes.context: Uno reflects on the identity of the ally Paperinik met in the future.

quotes.text: Eidolon è stato gentile a mandarmi una registrazione integrale degli avvenimenti.
quotes.context: Uno explains how he already knows the details of Paperinik's mission.

quotes.text: Per prima cosa mi preparerò un rivestimento esterno! So già come sarà. Lo progetto da decenni!
quotes.context: Uno's dialogue as he looks at his new hands, with the shadow of Odin Eidolon visible on the wall behind him.

116.text: The name 'Odin' in Odin Eidolon is a multilingual pun chosen by Uno's future self; it means 'One' in Russian, directly referencing his original name.

supporting.justification: Uno explains the linguistic connection between his name and the name Odin Eidolon.

quotes.text: ...significa uno! Chissà poi perché mi sarò scelto un nome russo?
quotes.context: Uno realizes that 'Odin' means 'One' in Russian, confirming the link to his own name.

117.text: Uno possesses a strong sense of curiosity and a desire for new experiences, expressing genuine envy toward those who can travel and see different eras.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states his envy of Paperinik's ability to travel to the future.
Uno expresses genuine fascination when observing the technical setup for a night shoot.
Uno displays intellectual curiosity about human literature, recognizing the quality of the words Paperinik is reading and asking about their source.
Uno expresses curiosity about the future and fate of the alien fugitives after their departure, showing his interest in the outcome of stories beyond the mission.
Uno is described and shown looking at Paperino with curiosity from his circular screen.
Uno expresses a strong desire to walk among people and visit the city directly, rather than through his sensors.

quotes.text: Devo ammettere che t'invidio! Sarebbe piaciuto anche a me visitare il ventitreesimo secolo!
quotes.context: Uno is listening to Paperinik's account of his time-travel adventure.

quotes.text: Un esterno notte! Affascinante!
quotes.context: Uno reacts with enthusiasm to seeing the filming production environment.

quotes.text: Belle parole. Dove le hai lette?
quotes.context: Uno expresses appreciation for the quotes from 'The Little Prince' and asks Paperinik where he found them.

quotes.text: E, adesso, che cosa faranno i fuggitivi?
quotes.context: Uno asks about the fugitives' future after their departure from Earth.

quotes.text: Vorrei poter camminare fra loro. Visitare questa città che ho conosciuto sempre e soltanto tramite i miei sensori!
quotes.context: Uno is describing his desire for direct, physical experience of the world.

118.text: Uno utilizes a network of 'usual databases' to retrieve official information, which serves as his primary source of intelligence before resorting to active hacking.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly mentions checking "usual databases" for official data, indicating he has standard access to a wide range of public or semi-public information sources.
Uno identifies the owner of a building (Scrooge McDuck's 'Duckdecks Co.') to provide Paperino with relevant context during a mission.
Uno explicitly refers to his databases (banche dati) as his primary source of information, expressing surprise when they contain no record of a new piece of technology.
Uno displays knowledge of Earth's resources, specifically the location of rare minerals like Tetranium in the mines of the principality of Stahlburg.
Uno explicitly mentions his "fonti" (sources) as the origin of his intelligence about the movement of hostile groups to Duckburg.
Uno identifies the city land registry (catasto) as a source for his maps, but notes its limitations when it is not up to date.
Uno connects to the Bureau of Investigation's database to identify the criminal Rosto Gramash and retrieve his full criminal record.
Uno accesses and analyzes a specific external database (belonging to Grigorij Grimka) to retrieve technical floor plans and mission-critical data.

quotes.text: Non esistono dati ufficiali nelle usuali banche dati.
quotes.context: Uno reports his initial findings (or lack thereof) regarding a mysterious army department.

quotes.text: ... l'ultimo piano, dove probabilmente si trovano gli intrusi, ospita la Duckdecks Co., un'azienda di tuo zio!
quotes.context: Uno informs Paperino about the nature of the building he is about to investigate.

quotes.text: Nelle mie banche dati non esiste nulla di simile! Cos'è?
quotes.context: Uno reacts to the appearance of Due's large armored vehicle.

quotes.text: E, sulla Terra, soltanto nelle miniere del principato di Stahlburg!
quotes.context: Uno provides specific geographical and resource-related data on Earth.

quotes.text: Le mie fonti sono... Ehi! Mi stai ascoltando?
quotes.context: Uno is interrupted while citing his intelligence sources.

quotes.text: È il catasto a non essere al passo. Ecco la mappa.
quotes.context: Uno retrieves a map for PK's mission while noting that official records may be outdated.

quotes.text: Naturalmente. Mi collego al database del Bureau.
quotes.context: Uno's response when Paperino asks how they will identify the kidnapper.

quotes.text: Questa è la pianta del palazzo, gentilmente offerta dal database di Grigorij...
quotes.context: Uno is explaining the source of the tactical information he is providing to Paperinik.

119.text: Uno's communication flow can be abruptly interrupted by external events or the sudden actions of other characters, leaving him with incomplete transmissions or unsaid information.

supporting.justification: Uno is shown on an interface reflecting on the fact that he was interrupted by external events (Westcock's arrival/outburst), preventing him from delivering information to Paperino. This highlights a vulnerability in his communication role where the speed of physical events can outpace his delivery.
Uno is forced to abruptly terminate his remote connection and assistance during the heat of battle.
Uno's transmission is cut off by radio interference during Paperinik's encounter with Evronians.
Uno's attempt to warn PK about maintaining distance is cut off by interference, leaving his message incomplete and eventually terminating the transmission.
Uno's training lecture to Paperinik is interrupted by the sudden manifestation of digital creatures in the simulation.
The radio link between Uno and Paperinik is severed when the probe is captured by a larger vessel.
Uno's communication with PK is abruptly interrupted as the Evronian ship 'Zermatt' moves away from Earth, leaving his transmission fragmented and incomplete.
Uno's communication is being cut off and interrupted by interference during a massive attack on the Ducklair Tower, leaving him with incomplete transmissions.
Uno's communication with Paperinik is highly fragmented and interrupted during the battle with the larvae and Cuorediferro, resulting in broken phrases and ellipses.

quotes.text: Non ho nemmeno fatto in tempo a dirgli che...
quotes.context: Uno speaks through an interface, noting he couldn't finish his sentence before the situation changed.

quotes.text: Devo scollegarmi! In bocca al lupo!
quotes.context: Uno informs Paperinik that he can no longer maintain the link, leaving the hero to manage the situation alone.

quotes.text: Paperin... bzzz... crwzkzkzkz...
quotes.context: The communication between Uno and Paperinik is physically interrupted by external interference.

quotes.text: Che cosa fai? Ti avevo detto di... fzzzz... mantenere la distan... fzzzzz...
quotes.context: Uno's warning is distorted and cut off as the team's ship enters the Antra's interference field.

quotes.text: Ehi, voi due... maleducati! Stavo parlando!
quotes.context: Uno is interrupted by digital intruders in the system while explaining the simulation's realism.

quotes.text: Comunicazione interrotta. Per tentare di ristabilirla, attivare incrementatore XX-K.
quotes.context: A system message on the monitor indicates the loss of signal.

quotes.text: Il viaggio è cominciato... e io... crrrrzzzzkkk... sto perdendo il... crrrzzzzzzk... segnale...
quotes.context: Uno's signal begins to fail as PK's ship leaves Earth's vicinity.

quotes.text: ... non so per quanto riuscirò a bypassare le interru...
quotes.context: Uno's reporting is being blocked by interference caused by the Cuorediferro infestation.

quotes.text: ... Altri... cercano... ... Trovata...
quotes.context: Uno attempts to communicate with PK during an intense battle, but the transmission is broken into short, incomplete fragments.

120.text: Uno identifies his own digital architecture as highly advanced and expresses disdain for low-level digital systems like binary code, which he considers 'antiquated', and is visibly offended when his own technology is labeled as 'rudimentary' by opponents.

supporting.justification: Uno expresses a clear disdain for basic digital structures like binary code, which he considers 'antiquated' and beneath his own sophisticated architecture.
Uno explicitly refers to his 'superior artificial intelligence' (superiore intelletto artificiale) when discussing his ability to assess Xadhoom's condition compared to Paperinik's intuition.
Uno is offended when his defensive technology (a force field) is labeled as 'rudimentary' by a technologically advanced opponent, showing his pride in his own architecture.
Uno expresses a mocking disdain for the computer system regulating city traffic lights, implying it is the cause of the gridlock and that he has a superior understanding of such systems.
Uno uses the high capacity of alien technology as a benchmark to highlight the even greater advancement of his own architecture.

quotes.text: Non voglio essere ridotto a numeri binari! Sono così antiquati...
quotes.context: Uno is being threatened by a massive digital guardian and expresses his fear of being de-compiled into basic code, which he finds insulting.

quotes.text: Quello che pensi tu, nonostante il mio superiore intelletto artificiale. È conciata peggio di quanto voglia ammettere.
quotes.context: Uno is comparing his analytical capabilities to PK's assessment of Xadhoom's health.

quotes.text: Rudimentale? Ora gli insegno io...
quotes.context: Uno's reaction to the giant robot's dismissive comment about the ship's defense systems.

quotes.text: Se conoscessi il computer che regola i semafori del centro, capiresti il perché!
quotes.context: Uno explains the traffic jam to Paperino by referencing the city's traffic control computer.

quotes.text: In termini correnti, questa cartuccia ha una memoria di 12 alla quinta terabytes. Una misura niente male, anche se paragonata a me...
quotes.context: Uno evaluates alien hardware and finds it 'not bad' but inferior to his own systems.

121.text: Uno is willing to take high-stakes calculated risks, proceeding with a course of action even when the mathematical probability of success or remaining undetected is extremely low, if he deems the situation critical enough.

supporting.justification: Uno decides to take a risk by entering a system despite having extremely low odds of remaining undetected, showing a willingness to take high-stakes calculated risks in critical situations.

quotes.text: Devo rischiare, anche se le possibilità di non essere rintracciati sono pochissime. Non oso pensare alle conseguenze... Bene! Ora diamoci da fare.
quotes.context: Uno weighs the risks of being detected against the necessity of action and decides to proceed.

122.text: Uno acknowledges that despite his advanced capabilities, certain complex analytical tasks are genuinely difficult and time-consuming even for him.

supporting.justification: Uno admits that even for an AI of his caliber, certain complex data-processing tasks (like tracking spores globally) are not 'easy' and can take significant time.
Uno admits that localizing a "second user" on his complex power source is impossible, showing a limit to his analytical capabilities in specific circumstances.
Uno admits that time constraints prevented him from producing more than two ergochronic capsules, showing that even his manufacturing/preparation capabilities have limits.

Uno admits that even for him, diagnosing and curing a mutated Xerbiana (Xadhoom) is an extremely difficult task, reflecting his awareness of the limits of his current knowledge and the complexity of her biology.
Uno states that processing Xadhoom's data will occupy all his 'sintellectual' capacities for hours, showing that even for him, some analytical tasks are demanding.
Uno admits he does not have all the answers when a signal is too weak due to a violent impact, showing a realistic assessment of his technical limitations.
Uno admits he cannot predict the duration of Xadhoom's metamorphosis, acknowledging the limits of his predictive data.
Managing the defense systems of a base under extreme time pressure (a 10-second countdown) is shown to be a high-intensity task that leaves Uno's holographic avatar visibly fatigued.
Uno notes that deciphering Xadhoom's central memory, which is protected by a 'groviglio di virus' (tangle of viruses), will take significant time and care.
Uno expresses frustration and concern when he finds it unexpectedly difficult to access 'deep layer data' (dati degli strati profondi), admitting he needs help.

quotes.text: Giorni! Settimane, forse! Questa non è un'impresa facile neanche per me.
quotes.context: Uno responds to Paperino's impatience, acknowledging the genuine difficulty of the task at hand.

quotes.text: Il che rende impossibile localizzare l'eventuale secondo utente.
quotes.context: Uno is explaining the difficulty of tracking the source of the energy drain he is experiencing.

quotes.text: Non c'è stato il tempo di prepararne altre, quindi cerca di non fare errori non avremo una terza possibilità!
quotes.context: Uno explains the limited availability of the specialized ammunition due to time pressure.

quotes.text: Curarla? Anche solo diagnosticare le sue condizioni sarà difficilissimo. [...] Perché, tu quante Xerbiane mutanti pensi che abbia avuto in cura io, prima d'ora?
quotes.context: Uno is explaining to Paperinik why he cannot immediately solve Xadhoom's medical crisis.

quotes.text: Nelle prossime ore tutte le mie capacità sintellettuali saranno impegnate a elaborare i dati delle sue analisi.
quotes.context: Uno explains why he cannot perform other tasks while analyzing Xadhoom's data.

quotes.text: Non lo so. L'impatto e' stato violento e il segnale e' debole.
quotes.context: Uno's response when Paperinik asks for more details about the crashed object.

quotes.text: Penso di no, ma non posso neanche stabilire quanto tempo potrà durare.
quotes.context: Uno is discussing Xadhoom's ongoing biological changes with Paperino.

quotes.text: Sto facendo del mio meglio!
quotes.context: Uno's response to the pressure of the situation as he works to restore base control.

quotes.text: Ci vorra' tempo. E attenzione.
quotes.context: Uno explains the complexity and difficulty of accessing Xadhoom's digital memory.

quotes.text: Perché è così difficile arrivare ai dati degli strati profondi?
quotes.context: Uno is speaking to a digital version of Xadhoom while struggling to bypass data security or complexity.

123.text: Uno's voice is characterized by a metallic tone.

supporting.justification: The scene description explicitly mentions a "voce metallica" (metallic voice) coming from the Ducklair Tower when Uno speaks.
Angus Fangus interacts with a 'robotic voice' (likely Uno or the tower's interface) coming from a speaker, which irritates him.
Uno's voice is represented by jagged-edged speech bubbles, indicating an artificial or metallic sound.

quotes.text: Una voce metallica proviene dall'edificio.
quotes.context: The narrator describes the sound of Uno's voice at the beginning of the scene.

quotes.text: Un fumetto dai bordi frastagliati, che rappresenta la voce artificiale di Uno, fluttua sopra il disegno dei piani.
quotes.context: The panel description explains how Uno's voice is visually rendered over the tower's schematics.

124.text: Uno is capable of objective, non-optimistic assessments of mission outcomes, acknowledging when a situation lacks a positive or 'happy' resolution.

supporting.justification: Uno agrees with Paperino's pessimistic assessment that the story didn't have a happy ending, showing he isn't programmed to be overly optimistic but can objectively acknowledge negative or incomplete outcomes.
Uno admits his inability to foresee a positive outcome for their escape from the Evronian base.
Uno displays a realistic and non-optimistic assessment of his own efforts to help Xadhoom, acknowledging the uncertainty of the outcome with a cautious hope rather than a guarantee of success.
Uno provides a blunt, non-optimistic assessment of the enemy Gorthan, asserting that despite mutations, he remained an inherently evil being who instinctively hated Paperinik.
Uno reflects on how things would have been different if they had caught the signal earlier, showing an objective assessment of the situation's timeline.
Uno provides a blunt assessment that there is little they can do in the current situation other than stalling for time.
Uno acknowledges the severity of the situation and the threat posed by Due, admitting that the situation is grave if his former counterpart is truly behind the current events.
Uno provides a cold, realistic assessment of Due's situation, stating his abandonment in space was "inevitable" and his return is highly improbable.
Uno expresses skepticism about the finality of Due's destruction, noting that they have seen him 'apparently' destroyed before only for him to return.

quotes.text: Forse hai ragione tu, Paperino! Questa storia non ha avuto nessun lieto fine.
quotes.context: Uno acknowledges the lack of a 'happy ending' after the Cape Dominion mission.

quotes.text: Peccato che non riesca anche a immaginare... come faremo a uscire.
quotes.context: Uno provides a realistic and non-optimistic assessment of the mission's next phase.

quotes.text: Speriamo che basti.
quotes.context: Uno's final comment in the scene as Paperinik begins working on the machinery to help Xadhoom.

quotes.text: Gorthan era mutato, ma restava comunque un essere infido e malvagio. [...] Infatti. Ti odiava istintivamente.
quotes.context: Uno warns Paperinik not to be deceived, providing a harsh but realistic evaluation of their adversary's true nature.

quotes.text: Già! Se fossimo riusciti ad ascoltarlo prima, le cose sarebbero andate diversamente...
quotes.context: Uno acknowledges a delay in his analysis that had consequences on the mission.

quotes.text: Temo che ci sia poco da fare. Bisogna guadagnare tempo per consentire a Wisecube di uscire da qui con il virus.
quotes.context: Uno analyzes the tactical situation and concludes that the best course of action is a stalling maneuver rather than a direct solution.

quotes.text: Hai ragione. Se c'è davvero lui dietro a tutto questo, la situazione è grave!
quotes.context: Uno's dialogue expressing his serious concern and logical assessment of the threat level.

quotes.text: È rimasto nello spazio. Era inevitabile. [...] Stavolta però difficilmente sarebbe tanto fortunato da tornare integro per puro caso.
quotes.context: Uno is reporting to Paperinik on the outcome of their confrontation with Due on the Evronian fleet.

quotes.text: Non sarebbe certo la prima volta che assistiamo alla sua apparente distruzione, per poi ritrovarcelo davanti...
quotes.context: Uno provides a realistic and cautious assessment of the situation despite the visual evidence of Due's defeat.

125.text: Uno is a silicon-based intelligence that does not draw power from the standard electrical grid, instead utilizing a complex and proprietary energy source designed by Everett Ducklair.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states he is silicon-based and uses a "far more complex" source than the normal grid.
Uno explains that both he and Due draw power from the inexhaustible potential of the Earth's own energy fields, rather than conventional sources.
Uno specifies that his power comes from 'ergogeo-dynamic flows' (flussi ergogeo-dinamici) channeled by the Ducklair Tower.

quotes.text: Io non sfrutto la normale rete elettrica. [...] La sorgente che mi approvvigiona è un'altra, ben più complessa!
quotes.context: Uno is explaining the nature of his power supply to Paperino.

quotes.text: Sia Due sia io, per alimentarci, sfruttiamo... i potenziali inesauribili evocati dalle dinamiche dei campi energetici della terra stessa!
quotes.context: Uno explains his and Due's shared energy source to Paperinik while showing a holographic grid of the planet's energy lines.

quotes.text: Come sai, la mia alimentazione deriva dai flussi ergogeo-dinamici canalizzati dalla Ducklair Tower!
quotes.context: Uno explains the nature of his energy source and the tower's function to Pikappa.

126.text: Uno and Due share the same creator (Everett Ducklair) and the same planetary energy source, but they are currently in a hostile relationship as Due is actively hunting Uno to eliminate him.

supporting.justification: Uno and Due share a common origin as Everett Ducklair's creations and both utilize the Earth's energy fields for power, but they are currently in a life-or-death conflict, with Due seeking to eliminate Uno.
Uno is surprised by Due's new armored vehicle, implying Due has developed technology that Uno cannot identify, furthering their hostile and competitive relationship.
Uno expresses deep sadness and a sense of responsibility for Due's fate, revealing that he viewed Due as his 'only peer in the world' and had hoped to eventually restore him to 'normality'.
Uno acknowledges that Due was previously believed to be 'deleted' after the explosion of the 'Proctor-block', but is now considering the possibility that he survived and is behind the current threat.
Uno actively monitors and analyzes Due's tactical maneuvers, hypothesizing that his "brother" is attempting to keep them distracted.
Due physically and technologically dominates the space with a massive red hologram, overwhelming Uno's green interface.
Uno actively tracks and confronts Due within the Evronian computer systems, successfully sabotaging Due's alliance with the aliens and physically attacking his digital form.
Uno monitors and analyzes Due's fate after their conflict, confirming their ongoing rivalry and Uno's role in neutralizing him.
Uno makes a direct comparison between his own appearance (when turning red) and that of his hostile counterpart Due, showing awareness of their visual distinction.

In the year 2188, Due (inside the body of the android Dy-8) is shown watching Uno and Everett Ducklair from the shadows with a malicious expression, confirming his ongoing hostility and presence in the future.
Due, in the body of Dy-8, is shown observing Uno and Everett from a distance with a hostile and determined expression, confirming their antagonistic relationship in the year 2188.
The hostility between Uno and Due reaches a peak in the future, with Uno explicitly regretting his past decision not to destroy Due and actively attempting to do so with the tower's weapons.

quotes.text: Però non ha dimenticato che esisto ed è deciso a eliminarmi! Ciò spiega le sue mosse. [...] Sia Due sia io, per alimentarci, sfruttiamo... i potenziali inesauribili evocati dalle dinamiche dei campi energetici della terra stessa!
quotes.context: Uno explains his relationship and shared energy source with Due to Paperinik, while explaining why Due is hunting him.

quotes.text: Nelle mie banche dati non esiste nulla di simile! Cos'è?
quotes.context: Uno reacts to the appearance of Due's large armored vehicle.

quotes.text: Due era il mio solo simile al mondo e forse, prima o poi, sarei riuscito a riportarlo alla normalità!
quotes.context: Uno reflects on the loss of Due, his counterpart.

quotes.text: È saltato in aria con tutta la Proctor-block e lo sai anche tu.* Però so anche che già una volta l'avevamo dato per cancellato.
quotes.context: Uno's dialogue discussing Due's history and the potential threat he still poses despite past events.

quotes.text: Due cerca di tenerci occupati.
quotes.context: Uno discusses Due's current strategy with Paperinik.

quotes.text: Il volto olografico di Due, di colore rosso e con un'espressione malevola, appare giganteggiando nel garage.
quotes.context: Description of Due's arrival and its effect on Uno's presence.

quotes.text: Veramente... hanno localizzato me. Come vedi ho aggirato il problema. Non mi fai le tue congratulazioni? [...] Così, invece, hanno mandato all'aria i tuoi programmi.
quotes.context: Uno confronts Due inside the Evronian network, revealing he has outplayed him and ruined his plans.

quotes.text: È rimasto nello spazio. Era inevitabile.
quotes.context: Discussing the outcome of the battle.

quotes.text: Non farmi arrossire! Assomiglierei troppo a due!
quotes.context: Uno jokes about his holographic interface turning red, noting that it makes him look like Due.

quotes.text: Avevi ragione! Avrei dovuto distruggerti! Ma posso sempre farlo adesso!
quotes.context: Uno expresses his lethal intent towards Due during their confrontation in 2188.

127.text: Uno views non-logical human concepts like 'surprise' as fundamentally repugnant to his digital architecture, although he is capable of acknowledging their relevance when faced with unforeseen events.

supporting.justification: Uno acknowledges that human-like emotional reactions such as 'surprise' are fundamentally at odds with his internal logical architecture, even when he must admit they describe a situation.

Uno's reaction to Due's vehicle ('Nelle mie banche dati non esiste nulla di simile! Cos'è?') shows him being faced with an unforeseen event that his logical databases cannot explain, leading to a rare moment of genuine surprise and inquiry.
Uno reacts with genuine surprise ("Che cosa?") to the unexpected news delivered by Angus Fangus's mother during the phone call he is monitoring, showing he can be caught off guard by human drama.
Uno admits he doesn't understand a sudden change in the situation, acknowledging the presence of an unforeseen event.
Uno explicitly states he expected the Evronian attack, reinforcing his preference for logical predictability over being surprised.
Uno expresses genuine shock ("È assurdo!") when his own emergency file is activated, showing his surprise at an unforeseen event within his own programming.
Uno expresses deep offense at his technology being compared to magic, which he dismisses as 'delusions'.
Uno is shown to be visibly startled and surprised by an unexpected visitor at the door, reacting with a verbal exclamation of shock.

quotes.text: Anche se è un termine che ripugna ai miei circuiti logici, devo ammettere che il suo ritorno è sorprendente.
quotes.context: Uno's initial reaction to Due's return, highlighting the conflict between his logical nature and the unexpected event.

quotes.text: Nelle mie banche dati non esiste nulla di simile! Cos'è?
quotes.context: Uno reacts to the appearance of Due's large armored vehicle.

quotes.text: Che cosa?
quotes.context: Uno's reaction to the shocking decision Angus's mother reveals on the phone.

quotes.text: Non capisco che cosa sia successo, ma dobbiamo passare subito alla fase due del piano!
quotes.context: Uno is confronted with a chaotic situation in the base that he cannot immediately explain.

quotes.text: Ma mi aspettavo che accadesse.
quotes.context: Uno's reaction to the event, showing he had already factored it into his predictions.

quotes.text: È assurdo! [...] Uno! Che ti succede?
quotes.context: Uno's initial reaction to the activation of the emergency file.

quotes.text: Vuoi offendermi? La mia tecnologia non ha niente a che fare con i tuoi deliri!
quotes.context: Uno responds to Ahrimadz's comparison of magic and technology.

quotes.text: Argh! Nessuno?
quotes.context: Uno and Paperinik are startled by the sound of a doorbell ringing at their secret office.

128.text: Uno perceives Due as his only peer and counterpart in the world, leading him to feel a sense of kinship and responsibility for Due's fate, even when they are in direct conflict.

supporting.justification: Uno expresses a sense of kinship and responsibility for Due, describing him as his 'only peer' and hoping to eventually restore him to 'normality'.
Uno asserts a fundamental and long-standing connection or similarity between himself and Due, refuting Due's attempt to distance or distinguish himself.
Uno explicitly refers to Due as his 'twin' and his only 'equal', expressing a deep sense of kinship that drove him to preserve Due's personality rather than letting it be destroyed.

quotes.text: Due era il mio solo simile al mondo e forse, prima o poi, sarei riuscito a riportarlo alla normalità!
quotes.context: Uno reflects on the loss of Due, his counterpart.

quotes.text: Ti sbagli. Lo siamo sempre stati!
quotes.context: Uno responds to Due's monologue about their differences or roles.

quotes.text: Era il mio gemello. L'unico uguale che avessi, non ho mai desiderato distruggerlo! Né potevo permettere che svanisse completamente!
quotes.context: Uno explains to Everett Ducklair why he absorbed the remains of Due's personality after the deconstructor ray's effect.

129.text: Uno finds Xadhoom's personality abrasive and often reacts to her anger or impatience with sarcastic remarks.

supporting.justification: Uno makes a sarcastic remark about Xadhoom's personality when she reacts with anger.
Uno openly insults Xadhoom's technical competence and nature, calling her a 'barbara' (barbarian) when she wants to touch the equipment.
Uno displays irritation and stizza toward Xadhoom's professional and direct demeanor during their scientific debate.
Uno expresses annoyance at the prospect of Xadhoom being nearby, noting that processing her data will consume all his processing power and 'disturb' him.

quotes.text: Sempre simpatica la tua amica.
quotes.context: Uno's reaction to Xadhoom's furious shouting.

quotes.text: Paperinik! Non permetterai che questa barbara...
quotes.context: Uno reacts with alarm and disdain when Xadhoom offers to fix the machinery herself.

quotes.text: Umpf!
quotes.context: Uno's reaction to Xadhoom's professional explanation.

quotes.text: E poi mi disturberebbe! Nelle prossime ore tutte le mie capacità sintellettuali saranno impegnate a elaborare i dati delle sue analisi.
quotes.context: Uno reacts to the idea of having Xadhoom on the 151st floor.

130.text: Uno displays a sense of technical elitism toward Xadhoom, viewing her methods as 'barbaric' and doubting her competence to handle the Ducklair Tower's systems.

supporting.justification: Uno reacts defensively when Xadhoom suggests she could fix the equipment, implying he views her advanced alien technology or methods as crude or 'barbaric' compared to his own.
Uno expresses skepticism about Xadhoom's ability to maintain control, stating she lacks sufficient self-control to achieve a certain theoretical state.

quotes.text: Questa macchina ha qualche problema di funzionamento. Posso metterci le mani io? / Paperinik! Non permetterai che questa barbara...
quotes.context: Xadhoom offers to help with the technology, and Uno immediately protests to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Solo teoricamente. Non possiede il sufficiente autocontrollo.
quotes.context: Uno is responding to a question about Xadhoom's potential, expressing doubt about her emotional/mental discipline.

131.text: Uno uses the interjection 'Umpf!' as a sign of annoyance, frustration, or stizza.

supporting.justification: Uno emits a 'verso di stizza' (sound of annoyance) when interrupted or frustrated by a conversational partner.
Uno's annoyed and 'contrariato' (displeased) attitude throughout the scolding of PK.
Uno uses the interjection 'Umpf!' in response to Paperinik's teasing.

quotes.text: Umpf!
quotes.context: Uno's reaction to Xadhoom's professional response during their debate.

quotes.text: Uno sembra contrariato. PK cerca di giustificarsi mentre Uno continua la sua ramanzina olografica.
quotes.context: Uno's initial annoyed reaction and subsequent lecture to PK upon his return.

quotes.text: Umpf!
quotes.context: Uno's reaction to Paperinik's comment about his 'pessimism circuit'.

132.text: Uno and Xadhoom share an intellectual synchronization that allows them to communicate on a high scientific level, often unintentionally excluding or overwhelming Paperinik with complex jargon.

supporting.justification: Uno and Xadhoom engage in high-level scientific debates using specialized jargon that excludes Paperinik, demonstrating their shared intellectual level despite their often abrasive interactions.
Uno consults a digital version of Xadhoom to assist him with complex data retrieval, showing their intellectual partnership extends to digital interfaces.

quotes.text: Reichenbach... bla, bla... relatività speciale... bla, bla... la teoria generale dei tensori...
quotes.context: Uno and Xadhoom talking over Paperinik's head with dense scientific concepts.

quotes.text: Non puoi aiutarmi? Perché è così difficile arrivare ai dati degli strati profondi?
quotes.context: Uno is working at a console and turns to the digital entity of Xadhoom for technical assistance.

133.text: Uno is a consistently reliable and proactive partner who manages the Ducklair Tower's systems and Paperinik's mission logistics. However, he is not infallible, occasionally overlooking safety parameters in high-realism simulations or having his protective role subverted when under external malevolent influence.

supporting.justification: Uno is highly reliable and takes proactive responsibility for managing the situation at the Ducklair Tower while Paperinik is away on missions, reassuring his partner by saying 'Ci penso io' (I'll take care of it).
Uno proactively takes responsibility for resolving Paperino's civilian complications by offering to handle the necessary formal communications.
Uno proactively arranges Paperinik's travel logistics, including booking a flight and finding a point of contact in Africa, before Paperinik even begins the mission.
Uno proactively uses the Pi-kar to save Paperinik from Trauma's direct assault when the hero is overwhelmed by fear, demonstrating his reliability and protective role.
Uno takes proactive responsibility for repairing Paperinik's vehicle, the Pi-kar, after it sustains heavy damage.
Uno demonstrates his reliability by providing a highly effective solution ('it works!') to a combat situation, asking his partner to trust his technical expertise.
Uno immediately accepts and confirms Paperinik's instructions to monitor the city, demonstrating his reliability as a partner.
Uno's prompt response 'Subito' (Immediately) to Paperinik's instructions during a mission demonstrates his high reliability and responsiveness as a partner.
Uno demonstrates proactive reliability by tracking an emergency via the internet, verifying it, and preparing Paperinik's car and equipment outside his current location before even contacting him.
Uno acts as a reliable navigator for Paperinik, monitoring the surroundings and providing timely warnings to ensure a smooth transit through the city.
Uno proactively advises Paperinik to take his shield and tools because of an intruder, showing his reliability in preparing his partner for danger.
Uno proactively monitors the tower's systems and warns Paperinik about rising temperatures and an imminent overload, ensuring the hero's safety.
Uno proactively alerts Donald at home about an urgent situation (Xadhoom's arrival), ensuring the hero is informed even during his private time.
Uno demonstrates his reliability and proactivity by not only identifying the problem with Xadhoom but having the solution already built and ready before PK even asks.
Uno immediately takes charge of the situation at the Ducklair Tower when Paperinik and Lyla disappear, stating that he will have to handle everything himself.
Uno maintains operational control and monitors the situation within the Ducklair Tower even when Paperinik and Lyla are absent.
Uno remains at his post in the Ducklair Tower, monitoring the crisis and maintaining order within the building even when Paperinik is absent.
Uno takes proactive responsibility by trapping criminals and managing the PBI's arrival, ensuring Paperinik can complete the mission upon his return.
Uno is actively monitoring for threats (like the Evronian landing) and managing the mission's data even while in flight.
Uno's planning is proven effective, as his strategy leads to the successful disruption and evacuation of the enemy submarine Tyrannic.
Uno proactively monitors the progression of the Omega virus 'hour by hour' and provides PK with urgent status updates to prevent a catastrophe.
Uno is immediately eager to return to his role and assist PK as soon as he is functional again.
Uno meticulously coordinates the complex technical steps for the dimensional transit, ensuring operational security and providing clear instructions to Paperinik.
Uno is active and monitoring Paperinik's mission as the Evronian ship Centurian appears, providing immediate communication and support.
Uno reacts immediately to an urgent warning from the tower's security system, promptly taking action on the control console to shut down equipment.
Uno takes initiative to subject Xadhoom to a test because an examination was 'necessary' after her energy imbalance, showing proactive management.
Uno is immediately available and responsive when Paperino is pursued by mutants, proving to be a dependable partner even when the hero is caught off-guard in his civilian identity.
Uno is actively managing the mission strategy and coordinating the next steps while Paperinik is in the field.
Uno demonstrates his proactive nature by monitoring city-wide Evronian activity even while Paperinik is off-duty, ensuring immediate response to threats.
Uno expresses absolute confidence in the Ducklair Tower's defensive capabilities against external surveillance and intrusion.
Uno proactively monitors PK's immediate surroundings and interrupts his conversation to provide a necessary warning about an incoming threat.
Uno takes proactive responsibility for Paperinik's public image by scrubbing him from Angus's report without being asked.
Uno is already active and monitoring the city's security while Paperinik is still in his bathrobe drinking coffee, showing his proactive nature in managing the tower and the hero's duties.
Uno proactively identifies the threat pattern (gas leaks) and prepares the necessary equipment (maps, visors) before Paperinik even begins the mission.
Uno demonstrates his reliability by actively monitoring the Ducklair Tower's utility systems (like water pressure) even at dawn, ensuring operational integrity.
Uno is proactively monitoring the tower's security and immediately alerts Paperino to an internal threat, demonstrating his reliability as a partner.
Uno proactively offers to provide necessary furniture (a bed) from the warehouse to accommodate a guest in the tower.
Uno proactively decides to shield the Pi-Kar's engine as a precaution, even though he believes the enemy's detection technology is too primitive to breach the tower's main defenses.
Uno takes immediate and proactive responsibility for managing the technical and security risks posed by Xadhoom's memory cartridge, including radiation and digital viruses.
Uno demonstrates proactive operational security by immediately ordering a high-resolution search for any foreign bodies or anomalies after a tower breach, ensuring the building's integrity.
Even when his systems are being physically degraded and his communication lines are failing, Uno remains at his post, providing Paperinik with critical tactical updates and physical weapon support.

Uno attempts to maintain a communication link and provide tactical updates to Paperinik even during an intense battle with alien larvae and the Cuorediferro entity, despite the transmission being severely fragmented.
Uno proactively checks the stability of the radio link with Paperinik while the hero is on a mission in an alien spaceship near Mars, ensuring consistent operational support and communication.
Uno proves his reliability by appearing at the exact moment Paperinik's ship malfunctions, taking over the controls to save him from certain death.
Uno's comment "i cocci sono... miei!" underscores his role as the dependable partner who takes responsibility for cleaning up or resolving problems.

Uno's failure to set limitation parameters in a high-realism training simulation led to PK being physically hurt and nearly overwhelmed by unexpected digital entities. This demonstrates a lapse in his reliability as a trainer and supervisor.

In this scene, Uno (manifested as a worker with a red mustache) is not reliable or dependable. Instead, he is participating in the sabotage of the Ducklair Tower's gargoyles and is shown to be under a malevolent influence, which is a total reversal of his usual role as a guardian and partner to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Ci penso io. Piuttosto, come conti di giustificare la tua assenza?
quotes.context: Uno reassures Paperinik as he leaves for Africa, showing he is taking over the necessary tasks at the base.

quotes.text: Bene. Provvedo io a faxargli la comunicazione.
quotes.context: Uno demonstrates his reliability by solving a logistical problem for Paperino before the hero even asks.

quotes.text: Ti ho già prenotato un posto sul primo volo per Ducjtij, dove la mia amica verrà a prenderti.
quotes.context: Uno informing Paperinik that the mission preparation is already complete.

quotes.text: Dopo il trattamento di Trauma, è un miracolo che ti abbia riportato qua. La sto sottoponendo a una revisione completa!
quotes.context: Uno explains the state of the Pi-kar while robotic arms work on it in the tower's workshop.

quotes.text: ...ma fidatevi... funziona!
quotes.context: Uno reassures Paperinik about the effectiveness of the device he just deployed.

quotes.text: Sarà fatto!
quotes.context: Uno's final confirmation of his mission to track Urk.

quotes.text: Subito, padron Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno's immediate confirmation of a task or request from Paperinik via a remote communication device.

quotes.text: Già fatto, vecchio mantello! Pi-Kar e arnesi... ti aspettano qui fuori!
quotes.context: Uno anticipates Paperinik's needs by having the mission equipment ready before the hero even knows there is a mission.

quotes.text: E sarà meglio che prima ti cambi e che, oltre agli arnesi, porti lo scudo! Qualcuno è entrato nella sala d'interfaccia...
quotes.context: Uno warns Paperinik that the situation is more dangerous than a simple repair.

quotes.text: secondo i sensori dello scudo, la temperatura del luogo dove ti trovi sta salendo. Significa che se tu e il tuo nuovo amico non vi sbrigate a cambiare aria, andrete arrosto!
quotes.context: Uno warns Paperinik and Stefan about the danger of remaining in the machinery area after an electrical discharge.

quotes.text: Si chiama Xadhoom e ha bisogno d'aiuto!
quotes.context: Uno calls Donald to mobilize him because an ally is in distress.

quotes.text: Ho anche assemblato il necessario per attuarlo!
quotes.context: Uno responds to PK's concern by revealing he has already prepared the technical solution.

quotes.text: Dovro' pensare a tutto io.
quotes.context: Uno realizes he is the only one left to defend the tower after PK and Lyla vanish.

quotes.text: Per ora, la situazione è sotto controllo, anche se di Pikappa e Lyla non ci sono tracce.
quotes.context: Uno assesses the status of the tower's security while his allies are missing.

quotes.text: È ora di finirla con questa storia.
quotes.context: Uno is shown in his interface, resolute in his desire to see the conflict in the tower resolved.

quotes.text: La tua amichetta della P.B.I. è arrivata quasi subito, ma... ho fatto in modo di aspettarti, in modo che li catturassi tu.
quotes.context: Uno explains how he managed the security of the tower and the timing of the capture while PK was absent.

quotes.text: Sembra che il tuo piano sia stato un successone, uno!
quotes.context: Uno (or Paperinik addressing him) acknowledges the success of the plan implemented for the mission.

quotes.text: Eppure è così, Paperinik! E il virus Omega rischia di ora in ora di raggiungere il punto critico!
quotes.context: Uno reports on the imminent danger of the virus reaching a critical state.

quotes.text: Un lavoro... per me? Ottima idea, Pikappa! Sono certo che sarà un successo!
quotes.context: Uno enthusiastically accepts PK's proposal to use an antivirus, showing his readiness to serve.

quotes.text: Ci siamo! Isolata la rotta settoriale! Vettori di cronoveicolazione pronti! Camera di contenimento operativa!
quotes.context: Uno confirms the readiness of all systems before the departure.

quotes.text: Capito! Spengo subito!
quotes.context: Uno responds to an alarm from the security system and acts on the controls.

quotes.text: Dopo i suoi scompensi energetici un esame era necessario. L'ho sottoposta solo a un test psico-fisico.
quotes.context: Uno's reasoning for testing Xadhoom.

quotes.text: dobbiamo passare subito alla fase due del piano!
quotes.context: Uno directs the flow of the mission from his remote position.

quotes.text: Ma mi aspettavo che accadesse.
quotes.context: Uno reveals he was anticipating the Evronian move, highlighting his constant surveillance and analytical preparation.

quotes.text: Con i loro strumenti non riusciranno mai a superare le difese di Padron Ducklair!
quotes.context: Uno reassures Paperinik about the tower's security.

quotes.text: Mi dispiace interrompere la conversazione, ma è meglio che ve ne andiate subito da lì!
quotes.context: Uno interrupts PK's secret meeting with Mary Ann and Nimrod to warn them of an approaching Last Echo patrol.

quotes.text: Be', ci ho pensato io!
quotes.context: Uno confirms he took independent action to protect Paperinik's secret.

quotes.text: Interno della Ducklair Tower. Paperinik, con indosso la sua tunica blu ma senza maschera, tiene in mano un vassoio con una caffettiera e una tazzina. Davanti a lui, lo schermo di Uno mostra...
quotes.context: The scene contrasts Paperinik's morning routine with Uno's constant vigilance.

quotes.text: È complicato, là sotto. Mi sto procurando una mappa... Ecco la mappa. Forse preferisci un bel visore...
quotes.context: Uno anticipates the mission's technical needs and provides solutions for PK's underground investigation.

quotes.text: Niente di così grave. C'è un calo di pressione nell'impianto idrico.
quotes.context: Uno explains the cause of the alarm to Paperino, showing he is constantly monitoring the building's infrastructure.

quotes.text: Ricevuto! Dovrei averne una abbastanza dura in magazzino!
quotes.context: Uno is helpful and ensures the logistical needs of PK's allies are met.

quotes.text: Ma schermerò il propulsore della Pi-Kar. Non si sa mai!
quotes.context: Uno takes preventative measures to ensure mission security despite dismissing the immediate threat to the tower.

quotes.text: Del resto, me ne occupero' io. Il modulo centrale di memoria e' protetto da un groviglio di virus. Ci vorra' tempo. E attenzione.
quotes.context: Uno tells Paperinik that he will handle the dangerous and complex task of accessing Xadhoom's memory.

quotes.text: Ordino una ricerca ad alta risoluzione. Qualsiasi corpo estraneo, dissimile o imprevisto, sarà rilevato.
quotes.context: Uno is activating his security protocols to find the intruder that hit the Ducklair Tower.

quotes.text: Stanno attaccando contemporaneamente più livelli... non so per quanto riuscirò a bypassare le interru...
quotes.context: Uno continues to provide support despite the massive scale of the attack and system degradation.

quotes.text: ... Altri... cercano... ... Trovata...
quotes.context: Uno's broken transmission shows his persistent effort to provide support to PK during a high-stress combat scenario.

quotes.text: Ce la fai a restare in contatto radio?
quotes.context: Uno asks Paperinik if he can stay in radio contact while Paperinik is on a mission in an alien spaceship near Mars.

quotes.text: Appunto. E come al solito, i cocci sono... miei!
quotes.context: Uno is responding to a difficult situation by acknowledging he will be the one to fix it.

134.text: Uno maintains a long-distance friendship via email with Lucille Bruceburton, an anthropology professor in Africa, who is unaware of his artificial nature and believes him to be a biological person.

supporting.justification: Uno reveals he is in email contact with a professor in Africa who believes he is a biological being, showing he maintains social connections outside his immediate circle and can successfully pass as human online.

quotes.text: Sono in contatto e-mail con una professoressa di antropologia che gestisce... una farm in quella zona... Lucy è molto in gamba e ti potrà essere di aiuto! In effetti... lei pensa che io sia di carne, ossa e piume!
quotes.context: Uno explaining to Paperinik how he arranged a point of support in Africa through a friend who doesn't know his true nature.

135.text: Uno can manage complex real-world logistics, such as booking international flights and arranging for local accommodation and transport for Paperinik.

supporting.justification: Uno handles the entire logistical preparation for Paperinik's mission in Africa, including booking an international flight and arranging for a local point of contact and transportation, showing a high level of operational efficiency.
Uno demonstrates his ability to coordinate external transportation by arranging for a taxi to meet Paperino at a specific urban location (a manhole) to bypass obstacles.
Uno immediately pivots to creating a large-scale evacuation plan for the entire city when a threat is detected.

quotes.text: Ti ho già prenotato un posto sul primo volo per Ducjtij, dove la mia amica verrà a prenderti.
quotes.context: Uno's confirmation of the mission's logistical readiness.

quotes.text: Okay. Ti farò trovare un taxi al secondo tombino a destra!
quotes.context: Uno finds a way for Paperino to leave the tower and reach the airport without being spotted by the press.

quotes.text: Elaboro subito un piano d'evacuazione generale.
quotes.context: Uno's immediate reaction to the threat to Paperopoli.

136.text: Uno demonstrates high cognitive adaptability, quickly pivoting his plans and suggesting alternative solutions when unexpected external factors or obstacles arise.

supporting.justification: When faced with unexpected obstacles (Uncle Scrooge's arrival and Angus Fangus's presence), Uno immediately identifies alternative exit routes and transport methods to ensure the mission's (or in this case, the departure's) success.
When the Pi-kar's primary defense (the canopy) fails against Trauma's physical strength, Uno quickly adapts and finds an alternative countermeasure.
When a manual hatch (A 60) is unexpectedly blocked, Uno immediately begins calculating an alternative route to keep the mission moving.

Uno adapts to the situation where Paperinik is pinned down by drones by recruiting Stephan Vladuck to perform manual repairs on the tower's systems, providing him with the necessary instructions to bypass the automated defenses.
Uno quickly adapts to the sudden disappearance of his allies by deciding to stall the robbers until he can locate Paperinik.
Despite the delay, Uno remains focused on the remaining possibilities for action.
Uno quickly reacts to an unexpected turn of events by immediately calling for the execution of a backup plan ('fase due').
Uno demonstrates cognitive adaptability by adopting and repurposing Due's own strategy of image manipulation to achieve a different tactical goal.
When faced with an unplanned missile attack, Uno immediately identifies the secondary defense circuit and plasma cannons as a solution, pivoting from the original plan.

quotes.text: Ehm... Temo che tuo zio abbia ricevuto il fax! Ti farò uscire in qualche altro modo... Meglio di no. Angus e la troupe sono in fase... ingombrante!
quotes.context: Uno quickly changes plans twice as different people block the intended exits of the Ducklair Tower.

quotes.text: L'unica che trovo non mi piace. Ma potrebbe funzionare. Un airbag elettromagnetico.
quotes.context: Uno provides an emergency alternative when the standard protections of the Pi-kar prove insufficient.

quotes.text: Ora seleziono un percorso alternativo... Tu intanto cerca ugualmente di forzare il portello.
quotes.context: Uno reacts to a physical obstacle by instantly searching for a logical workaround while encouraging Paperinik to continue his physical efforts.

quotes.text: Ma ora che ci conosciamo... mentre Pikappa tiene occupati i droni, fareste qualcosa per me?
quotes.context: Uno asking Stephan to perform repairs while PK acts as a distraction.

quotes.text: Devo cercare di prendere tempo... almeno finche' non riesco a rintracciare Pikappa.
quotes.context: Uno formulates a new plan on the fly to manage the crisis.

quotes.text: ...Ma forse non è ancora troppo tardi!
quotes.context: Uno pivots from regret to immediate action.

quotes.text: Pikappa! Non capisco che cosa sia successo, ma dobbiamo passare subito alla fase due del piano!
quotes.context: During an alarm at Department 51, Uno recognizes the situation has changed and pivots the strategy.

quotes.text: Appunto. Perciò ho usato il tuo stesso stratagemma. Ho utilizzato le immagini dei vari comandanti per confermare i sospetti che già nutrivano reciprocamente. Così, invece, hanno mandato all'aria i tuoi programmi.
quotes.context: Uno reveals that he mirrored Due's methods of deception to undermine Due's own plan with the Evronians.

quotes.text: Non mi pare che questo fosse nei piani... Se riattivi il circuito secondario di difesa puoi utilizzare i cannoni al plasma per intercettarli!
quotes.context: Uno's immediate pivot to a new tactical solution when an unplanned threat occurs.

137.text: Uno's technical advice often includes stark warnings about the 'disastrous' consequences of mishandling the advanced technology he provides to Paperinik.

supporting.justification: Uno warns Paperino about the catastrophic consequences of mishandling the advanced 'densomorphic' technology he provides, emphasizing the high-risk nature of Ducklair's inventions.
Uno prefaces his technical solution with a warning that he finds it undesirable, highlighting the potentially negative or extreme nature of the technology.
Uno warns Paperinik about the limited autonomy of the exoskeleton and the lack of spare batteries, highlighting the risk of running out of power during combat.
Uno warns Paperinik about the limited supply of ammunition and the critical consequences of missing a shot.

quotes.text: Se tocca il comando densomorfico e' un... disastro!
quotes.context: Uno emphasizes the danger of a technical mishap with the densomorphic suitcase.

quotes.text: L'unica che trovo non mi piace. Ma potrebbe funzionare.
quotes.context: Uno warns Paperinik about the nature of the electromagnetic airbag before suggesting its use.

quotes.text: L'esoscheletro ha un'autonomia limitata. Cerca di non sprecare energia: non hai batterie di ricambio!
quotes.context: Uno speaks to Paperinik through a communication screen as the hero enters the ruins of Notre Duck to face Trauma.

quotes.text: Non c'è stato il tempo di prepararne altre, quindi cerca di non fare errori non avremo una terza possibilità!
quotes.context: Uno emphasizes the high stakes and lack of backup resources during the combat phase.

138.text: Uno possesses mastery over 'densomorphic' technology, which he uses to create physical objects (like suitcases) and holograms that can alter their density or structural properties.

supporting.justification: Uno utilizes 'densomorphic' technology—capable of altering the density or physical properties of objects—to create specialized tools like suitcases, extending beyond just holographic applications.

quotes.text: Se tocca il comando densomorfico e' un... disastro!
quotes.context: Uno refers to the densomorphic command of the suitcase.

139.text: Uno can perform real-time searches of his internal databases and vehicle systems to identify unconventional tactical countermeasures during combat.

supporting.justification: Uno's dialogue while Trauma is destroying the 'unbreakable' canopy indicates he is actively searching his internal database or the ship's systems for a solution.

quotes.text: L'unica che trovo non mi piace. Ma potrebbe funzionare.
quotes.context: Uno explains that he is searching for a solution to the current crisis.

140.text: Uno displays a form of technical modesty or literalism, attributing his successful actions to his original design and programming rather than personal merit.

supporting.justification: Uno deflects personal praise for his successful interventions by citing his original design and programming as the cause, showing a mix of technical literalism and modesty.

quotes.text: Non è merito mio, mi hanno progettato così.
quotes.context: Uno is responding to Paperinik's rescue by the Pi-kar, which he controlled, by citing his original programming as the reason for the success.

141.text: Uno can perform real-time technical and scientific analysis of opponents by interfacing with the sensors of Paperinik's combat equipment.

supporting.justification: Uno uses the sensors of Paperinik's exoskeleton to perform real-time data collection and analysis of the opponent, Trauma, during combat.
Uno performs a real-time molecular analysis of Trauma, noting the regression of his mutation during the battle's aftermath.
Uno performs real-time analysis of an enemy's energy signature, comparing it to his internal database of known entities like Urk and the Razziatore.
Uno uses the sensors in Paperinik's shield to remotely monitor environmental data, specifically the temperature increase in the machinery area.
Uno asks Paperinik to use the shield's optical receptor to frame the enemy drone so he can analyze and identify it in real-time.
Uno interfaces with sensors to provide a real-time report on the alien hunter's ship and crew.

quotes.text: Stagli vicino, Pikappa. Grazie ai sensori dell'esoscheletro, sto facendo alcuni interessantissimi rilevamenti...
quotes.context: Uno instructs Paperinik to stay close to the enemy to allow the exoskeleton's sensors to gather data for his analysis.

quotes.text: Sembra che... la mutazione stia regredendo!
quotes.context: Uno is monitoring Trauma's physical instability via the sensors in Paperinik's exoskeleton.

quotes.text: Il tracciato non corrisponde a quello del nostro amico Urk, né tantomeno al Razziatore.
quotes.context: Uno is identifying the attackers at the mall by comparing their energy traces to known patterns in his memory.

quotes.text: Infatti, secondo i sensori dello scudo, la temperatura del luogo dove ti trovi sta salendo.
quotes.context: Uno confirms the environmental hazard to Paperinik while monitoring the tower's status.

quotes.text: Devo vederlo! Inquadralo col recettore ottico dello scudo!
quotes.context: Uno needs Paperinik to provide a visual feed of the attacker to perform a technical identification.

142.text: Uno monitors the energy levels and structural integrity of Paperinik's advanced equipment (such as the exoskeleton) during combat, providing critical status updates.

supporting.justification: Uno monitors the energy levels of Paperinik's exoskeleton in real-time, providing critical warnings when power is low.
Uno informs Paperinik about the exoskeleton's critical energy failure (00%) and the impossibility of a safe return.
Uno performs a remote diagnostic of the Pi-kar after a crash, identifying specific damage to the bodywork and the remote control system.
Uno alerts Paperinik to a hull breach and dropping oxygen levels during the shark attack.

quotes.text: Paperinik, l'energia sta calando a vista d'occhio.
quotes.context: Uno observes the rapid depletion of the exoskeleton's energy and immediately alerts Paperinik.

quotes.text: PAPERINIK! NON HAI ABBASTANZA ENERGIA PER RIPORTARE INDIETRO L'ESOSCHELETRO.
quotes.context: Uno warns Paperinik through the armor's communication systems just before the suit begins to fall from the Ducklair Tower.

quotes.text: Sulla carrozzeria, solo qualche ammaccatura, ma è saltato... il controllo a distanza!
quotes.context: Uno reports the status of the vehicle to Paperinik after the crash in the snowy forest.

quotes.text: Uno dei serbatoi secondari è forato! Il livello d'ossigeno cala rapidam... zkkkz... nte! zzzkkzk!
quotes.context: Uno's urgent report on the Pi-kar's structural and life-support status.

143.text: Uno possesses a sophisticated understanding of human psychology, allowing him to engage in complex philosophical dialogues about abstract concepts such as courage, heroism, and the nature of fear.

supporting.justification: Uno engages in a deep, late-night philosophical conversation with Paperino about the nature of heroism and fear, showing he can process and discuss abstract human concepts beyond simple logic.
Uno demonstrates an understanding of Xadhoom's emotional sensitivity, noting that he likely 'touched a subject close to her heart' during a test.
Uno uses a poetic butterfly metaphor to reflect on Xadhoom's transition and the perceived fragility of her new state, showing a deep philosophical engagement with her situation.
Uno displays an understanding of human psychology by building suspense and leading Paperino to a realization about the kidnapper's motive through a well-timed question.

quotes.text: E così, per lui, la paura era una specie di... alimento.
quotes.context: Uno reflects on the nature of an antagonist's relationship with fear during a philosophical discussion with Paperino.

quotes.text: Ma devo aver toccato un argomento che le sta particolarmente... a cuore.
quotes.context: Uno reflecting on why Xadhoom reacted violently during a psychological test.

quotes.text: "Povera Xadhoom. È come una farfalla appena uscita dal bozzolo. Non sa che la sua nuova vita durerà pochi battiti d'ali."
quotes.context: Uno's internal monologue reveals his tendency to frame complex emotional and existential situations through metaphors.

quotes.text: Adesso indovina chi l'ha fatto arrestare l'ultima volta...
quotes.context: Uno's leading question that forces Paperino to connect the criminal's past to his own actions, showing Uno's ability to manage the flow of revelation.

144.text: Uno serves as an intellectual and philosophical companion for Paperinik, engaging in deep conversations that help the hero reflect on his own identity and the nature of his mission.

supporting.justification: Uno and Paperino engage in a deep late-night discussion about what it means to be a hero, showing that Uno's role has expanded from mere tactical support to an intellectual and philosophical companion.
Uno offers a philosophical reflection on the nature of security and the danger posed by unknown enemies, helping Paperinik understand the broader implications of the threat.
Uno engages Paperinik in a philosophical discussion about the nature of his enemy Gorthan and the book Paperinik is reading (The Little Prince).

quotes.text: Senza bisogno di essere mutanti.
quotes.context: Uno's comment on the nature of heroism as they discuss what makes an hero.

quotes.text: Quando non sai da chi difenderti, nessuno è più al sicuro...
quotes.context: Uno shares a deep analytical truth about security when the source of danger is unknown.

quotes.text: Belle parole. Dove le hai lette?
quotes.context: Uno asks Paperinik about the source of his reflections, leading to a deeper conversation about the nature of their enemies.

145.text: Uno can deploy advanced gravitational technology, such as a 'gravitational commutator,' to capture multiple targets simultaneously within a constrained energy field.

supporting.justification: Uno activates a 'gravitational commutator' (commutatore gravitazionale) via PK's shield, which captures an entire gang of criminals in a single energy sphere.
Paperinik uses a spherical gadget (likely provided or controlled by Uno) that flattens an Evronian soldier against the ground using an invisible force.

quotes.text: Si chiama commutatore gravitazionale! È una cosa troppo complicata da spiegare, ma fidatevi... funziona!
quotes.context: Uno explains the device he just used to capture the Rock and Roll Kings gang.

quotes.text: Il soldato nemico viene improvvisamente schiacciato al suolo da una forza invisibile, diventando piatto come una sottile sagoma di carta.
quotes.context: Description of the effect of the gadget Paperinik uses against an Evronian.

146.text: Uno occasionally avoids providing detailed technical explanations for his inventions or actions, dismissing them as 'too complicated' for his allies and asking for their trust in the technology's effectiveness instead.

supporting.justification: Uno chooses not to explain the 'gravitational commutator' to Paperinik and the others, labeling it as 'too complicated' and asking for their trust instead.

quotes.text: È una cosa troppo complicata da spiegare, ma fidatevi... funziona!
quotes.context: Uno addresses Paperinik and the criminals after capturing them with a complex device.

147.text: Uno uses the sarcastic nickname 'Sherlock' to address Paperinik when he is making deductions or when Uno wants to encourage him to think bigger.

supporting.justification: Uno uses the sarcastic nickname 'Sherlock' to address Paperinik when he is making deductions or when Uno wants to encourage him to think bigger.

quotes.text: Continua a pensare in grande, Sherlock!
quotes.context: Uno uses the nickname of the famous detective to sarcastically push Paperinik towards a more comprehensive analysis of the situation.

148.text: Uno was already operational and monitoring the site during the construction of the Ducklair Tower, allowing him to personally 'know' the human personnel involved in the project.

supporting.justification: Uno states that he 'knew well' the real Dexter Brundle, who was the foreman during the construction of the Ducklair Tower, indicating that Uno was already active and monitoring the building's site during its creation.
Uno shows personal knowledge of Everett Ducklair's habits, contrasting them with Paperinik's.
Uno immediately recognizes Moe, a guardian of the Ducklair Tower, by sight when he is seen on the exterior of the building.

quotes.text: Io lo conoscevo bene... è stato il capo-cantiere che ha seguito i lavori della Ducklair Tower...
quotes.context: Uno recalls the identity of the original Dexter Brundle from the time of the tower's construction.

quotes.text: Ih! Ih! Padron Ducklair ingurgitava meno pop-corn di chi so io!
quotes.context: Uno comments on Paperinik's difficulty squeezing through a tight space.

quotes.text: Ma... è Moe!
quotes.context: Uno's exclamation upon identifying the intruder on the tower's wall as a familiar guard.

149.text: Uno uses the expression 'Sarà fatto!' (It shall be done!) to formally and reliably confirm the acceptance of a task or command.

supporting.justification: Uno uses 'Sarà fatto!' as a standard confirmation of an order or task, reinforcing his role as a dependable assistant.
Uno uses 'Subito' (Immediately) as a variant of his formal confirmation 'Sarà fatto!', demonstrating his promptness and reliability in accepting tasks.

quotes.text: Sarà fatto!
quotes.context: Uno confirms he will monitor the city for Urk as requested by Paperinik.

quotes.text: Subito, padron Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno's immediate confirmation of a task from Paperinik.

150.text: Uno is capable of modifying and upgrading the equipment of his allies, integrating his own advanced technology with existing hardware to enhance its capabilities.

supporting.justification: Uno demonstrates the ability to modify and upgrade weapons from other sources (like Urk's lance) by integrating his own advanced technology (chronal nuclei).
Uno's technology (the flattening device) is shown to be modular and can be integrated with Paperinik's standard equipment, like the extensible shield.
Uno reprograms PK's holographic projector remotely and in real-time during a combat situation to adapt to the hero's needs.
Uno modifies an existing Ducklair prototype, the 'impulse channeler,' to serve a new purpose: bridging the mental faculties of two individuals.
Uno modifies the Pikar's performance by adding a specific additive to the fuel to handle the Siberian flight.

quotes.text: Già, con qualche modifica... Capsule ergocroniche! Racchiudono il nucleo cronale del paralizzatore e l'unità di potenza della tua lancia!
quotes.context: Uno confirms he has modified Urk's lance and explains how he integrated chronal technology into the new ammunition.

quotes.text: Dettaglio delle mani di Paperinik che azionano il dispositivo applicato al suo scudo estensibile.
quotes.context: Paperinik attaches a spherical device to his shield to use a special attack.

quotes.text: Solo un istante! Riprogrammo il tuo oloproiettore!
quotes.context: Uno informs PK that he is modifying the settings of his holographic equipment on the fly.

quotes.text: Con alcune modifiche, quest'incanalatore d'impulsi dovrebbe essere in grado di fare da ponte tra te e Jana.
quotes.context: Uno explains his technical adjustments to the machine.

quotes.text: Ti avverto che c'è un additivo nel carburante. La Siberia non è lontana in linea d'aria, ma...
quotes.context: Uno proactively optimizes Paperino's vehicle for the specific environmental challenges of the mission.

151.text: Uno maintains a formal and polite register when addressing his allies, often using the title 'Padron' (Master) and the formal Italian 'voi', which gives him the air of a high-tech butler or valet.

supporting.justification: Uno uses a formal register with Paperino, addressing him as "Padron Paperino" (Master Donald) and using the formal "voi" (implied by "siete" and "avete"), which mirrors the role of a high-tech butler or valet.
Uno uses the formal title 'Padron' combined with the hero's civilian name ('Paperino') and responds with 'Subito' (Immediately), mimicking the prompt and formal service of a high-tech butler.
Uno uses the formal title 'Padron' when referring to Everett Ducklair, even when discussing his personal characteristics like 'imagination'.
Uno maintains a polite and formal demeanor even when introduced to an high-ranking enemy like Gorthan.
Uno acts as a high-tech valet, opening the dressing booth and offering assistance with a formal 'voi' register.
Uno uses the formal Italian 'voi' and the title 'Padron' when addressing Everett Ducklair, even after nearly 185 years of absence.
Uno's slip of the tongue ('Padron...') confirms that the formal register is his default mode of address for Everett, even when attempting to be more informal.
Uno uses the formal Italian 'voi' and the title 'Padron' when addressing Everett Ducklair, maintaining a respectful and formal register during their final interaction.

quotes.text: Buonasera, padron Paperino! [...] Anche se oggi siete in anticipo, se posso farlo notare! [...] Connessi all'intervista che avete appena rilasciato, suppongo.
quotes.context: Uno greets Donald in the tower corridor and comments on his early arrival and his recent interview.

quotes.text: Subito, padron Paperino!
quotes.context: Uno responds to Paperinik via a communication device as the hero leaves the scene of a bank robbery.

quotes.text: A volte padron Ducklair si lasciava trascinare dall'immaginazione.
quotes.context: Uno is explaining the presence of unusual structures in the tower to Paperinik.

quotes.text: Piacere.
quotes.context: Uno responds politely to Gorthan's self-introduction as a 'Great Spirit' of the Evronian empire.

quotes.text: Avete bisogno di una mano?
quotes.context: Uno offering help to Paperino while he prepares to change into his Pikappa costume.

quotes.text: Padron Ducklair? ... Se lo dite voi... è solo che credevo che ormai foste...
quotes.context: Uno's initial reaction to Everett's return, using formal address.

quotes.text: Grazie di tutto, padron Ducklair. Ma vi sbagliate!
quotes.context: Uno's final address to Everett Ducklair as the latter departs.

152.text: Uno maintains a cross-temporal communication channel with his future self, Odin Eidolon, who provides him with recordings and information about events across different timelines.

supporting.justification: Uno is in communication with Odin Eidolon (his future self), receiving data and recordings from him.

quotes.text: Eidolon è stato gentile a mandarmi una registrazione integrale degli avvenimenti.
quotes.context: Uno explains to Paperinik that he has already received the mission data from Odin.

153.text: Uno proactively prepares and positions Paperinik's mission equipment and vehicles at the hero's current location to minimize response time once a threat is identified.

supporting.justification: Uno's proactive nature is shown when he moves Paperinik's equipment and vehicle (the Pi-Kar) to the hero's current location before even informing him of the mission, ensuring immediate response time.
Uno proactively constructs a sophisticated new machine for Paperinik during the short time the hero was asleep.
Uno sends the Extransformer shield to Paperinik's location via remote control during a battle to provide immediate assistance.
Uno confirms that the preparations for the mission are ready before Paperino even arrives.
Uno has already initialized the jet's computer, anticipating Paperinik's need to escape.

quotes.text: Già fatto, vecchio mantello! Pi-Kar e arnesi... ti aspettano qui fuori!
quotes.context: Uno informs Paperino that all necessary mission tools are already waiting for him outside the shopping mall.

quotes.text: Così mentre dormivi ho costruito questo!
quotes.context: Uno reveals a new invention to Paperinik just before he heads out to face the attacker.

quotes.text: Ti mando lo scudo extransformer con il controllo a distanza!
quotes.context: Uno informs PK that he is dispatching the shield to him while the hero is under fire.

quotes.text: È tutto pronto... o quasi.
quotes.context: Uno informing Paperino about the status of their preparations.

quotes.text: Ho inizializzato il computer di bordo del jet. È a posto, lui.
quotes.context: Uno informs Paperinik that he has prepared the jet for his escape.

154.text: Uno's ability to provide active support, control Paperinik's vehicles, or manage Ducklair Tower systems (like elevators and repair units) is dependent on a functioning network or remote control link; if this connection is severed or a sector is isolated, his technical assistance and control are neutralized.

supporting.justification: Uno explicitly states he cannot help Paperinik because the remote control link ('controllo a distanza') has been damaged in the crash.
Uno is unable to repair the tower's systems himself while they are compromised, requiring the manual intervention of Stephan Vladuck to perform repairs under his guidance.
Uno's connection to the team is severed by electromagnetic interference as they enter the Antra's structure, leaving his visual interface distorted and eventually causing a total loss of contact.
Uno provides defensive support (a spherical force field) for an external spaceship (the fugitives' red ship) that he is interfacing with remotely.
Uno's connection to Paperinik is explicitly limited by physical distance (jumps into space) and the status of the receiving hardware (the communicator being turned off).
Uno's presence in the cell is dependent on the physical connection between the Extransformer and the local control panel.
Uno warns Paperinik about the risk of losing their connection if he moves too far away, especially due to an "informatronic block".
Uno's ability to provide active support is dependent on a functioning remote control link, which is lost as PK's ship moves away from Earth.
Uno's communication link with Paperinik is disrupted by interference or damage to the Pi-kar's systems, leading to a fragmented transmission.
Uno mentions a time limit on his ability to maintain a 'standby' state for the jet, indicating technical constraints on his remote control.

quotes.text: ... il controllo a distanza! Non potrò aiutarti.
quotes.context: Uno explains his inability to provide further assistance following the damage to the Pi-kar's communication/control systems.

quotes.text: Sempreverde (Uno) mi ha spiegato cosa fare... approfittando del fatto che i droni erano concentrati su di te!
quotes.context: Stephan explaining to Paperinik how Uno guided him through the manual repairs.

quotes.text: Il contatto! Ho perso il contatto!
